basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Kahf — “The Cave” — unfolds four great stories bound by a single thread: detachment from the world and refuge in God. The People of the Cave, young believers fleeing for their faith, whom God caused to sleep for centuries, turning them now to the right and now to the left, under the guard of a dog stretched out on the threshold; the two garden owners, one of whom, drunk with his wealth, said “I do not think this will ever perish” until his harvest was destroyed; Moses and al-Khiḍr, the journey of hidden wisdom — the scuttled boat, the slain boy, the rebuilt wall, “I did it not of my own accord”; and Dhū-l-Qarnayn, who reached the setting and the rising of the sun and raised the barrier against Gog and Magog. The refrain runs through the sūra: “wealth and children are the adornment of the life of this world, but the enduring good deeds (al-bāqiyāt al-ṣāliḥāt) are better in reward and in hope”; and it culminates: “if the sea were ink for the words of my Lord, it would run dry before them.” Qushayrī makes of the Sleepers those who became extinct to themselves so as to subsist by God alone, sheltered in the shade of His refuge in the cave of intimacy; and of al-Khiḍr the keeper of the knowledge from God (ʿilm ladunnī), infused without the toil of seeking, whose strength lies in its very distance from all proof.
themes: refuge in the cave of intimacy
the adornment of the world and the enduring deeds

versets.ar: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَنْزَلَ عَلَىٰ عَبْدِهِ الْكِتَابَ وَلَمْ يَجْعَل لَّهُ عِوَجًا
versets.tr: al-ḥamdu li-llāhi lladhī anzala ʿalā ʿabdihi l-kitāba wa-lam yajʿal lahu ʿiwajan
versets.trad: Praise be to God, who has sent down the Book upon His servant and has placed in it no crookedness!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī links the praise at once to the story of the People of the Cave: those who became extinct to themselves so as to subsist by God alone. If praise is thanksgiving, then the sending down of the Book is the noblest of graces, for the message of the Beloved with the beloved holds the highest place. And if He names him “His servant,” that too is a signal favor: to name someone His servant is to set him among His intimates. The Prophet merits this name because he was extinct to his own portions, devoted wholly to God by the fulfillment of His rights.

versets.ar: قَيِّمًا لِّيُنذِرَ بَأْسًا شَدِيدًا مِّن لَّدُنْهُ وَيُبَشِّرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ الَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ الصَّالِحَاتِ أَنَّ لَهُمْ أَجْرًا حَسَنًا
versets.tr: qayyiman li-yundhira baʾsan shadīdan min ladunhu wa-yubashshira l-muʾminīna lladhīna yaʿmalūna ṣ-ṣāliḥāti anna lahum ajran ḥasanan
versets.trad: An upright Book, to warn of a terrible rigor that may come from Him, and to announce to the believers who do righteous deeds that a fair reward awaits them,
versets.commentaire: “Upright” (qayyim): preserved from contradiction, a mighty Book from a mighty Lord. The “terrible rigor,” says Qushayrī, is for the near term separation, for the deferred term burning; or more subtly, subsistence far from God and the trial of His wrath. As for the righteous deed, it is that which deserves acceptance, performed as it was commanded, marked by pure sincerity; and the fair reward is that which never reminds the servant of his shortfall and veils from his eyes the flaws of his deed.

mots.racine: ب أ س / ش د د

versets.ar: مَّاكِثِينَ فِيهِ أَبَدًا
versets.tr: mākithīna fīhi abadan
versets.trad: [Paradise] wherein they shall abide forever,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the glad tiding here is that these graces are perpetual, never cut off. The permanence of the dwelling answers the permanence of the gift: the eternity of the Garden reflects the eternity of the One who gives.

versets.ar: وَيُنذِرَ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا اتَّخَذَ اللَّهُ وَلَدًا
versets.tr: wa-yundhira lladhīna qālū ttakhadha llāhu waladan
versets.trad: and to warn those who say: “God has taken a child!”
versets.commentaire: According to Qushayrī, the glad tiding of the previous verse is greater still than this warning, for the believer knows that God does not forgive that anything be associated with Him, but forgives all else to whom He wills. The word ascribing a child to God is the root of every falsehood that will be condemned.

versets.ar: مَّا لَهُم بِهِ مِنْ عِلْمٍ وَلَا لِآبَائِهِمْ ۚ كَبُرَتْ كَلِمَةً تَخْرُجُ مِنْ أَفْوَاهِهِمْ ۚ إِن يَقُولُونَ إِلَّا كَذِبًا
versets.tr: mā lahum bihi min ʿilmin wa-lā li-ābāʾihim kaburat kalimatan takhruju min afwāhihim in yaqūlūna illā kadhiban
versets.trad: They have no knowledge of it, neither they nor their fathers. Monstrous is the word that comes from their mouths; they speak naught but a lie!
versets.commentaire: Their hideous word, says Qushayrī, is the fruit of their ignorance of God's oneness; they inherited it from their forefathers, and the serpent begets only a serpent. Their word was “monstrous” in sin because it was base in meaning. And he adds a fine warning: whoever utters that for which no leave was given to him crowds the ranks of such people, and whoever speaks of this Way before its time falls into their throng.

mots.racine: ك ب ر / ك ل م

versets.ar: فَلَعَلَّكَ بَاخِعٌ نَّفْسَكَ عَلَىٰ آثَارِهِمْ إِن لَّمْ يُؤْمِنُوا بِهَٰذَا الْحَدِيثِ أَسَفًا
versets.tr: fa-laʿallaka bākhiʿun nafsaka ʿalā āthārihim in lam yuʾminū bi-hādhā l-ḥadīthi asafan
versets.trad: Perhaps you will consume yourself with grief over their conduct, if they do not believe in this message.
versets.commentaire: Out of excess of tenderness, says Qushayrī, the Prophet was seized with sorrow at their refusal to believe; so God lightened the matter for him by what resembles a reproach in appearance, as if to say: why all this? Their refusal leaves, in Our reckoning, no trace, and the Religion suffers no harm from it. More deeply, He made him witness the running of the decree, and taught him that, though their unbelief is forbidden by the Law, it is in truth willed by the Real.

mots.racine: ب خ ع / ن ف س
mots.def: bakhaʿa: to kill oneself with grief.

versets.ar: إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا مَا عَلَى الْأَرْضِ زِينَةً لَّهَا لِنَبْلُوَهُمْ أَيُّهُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا
versets.tr: innā jaʿalnā mā ʿalā l-arḍi zīnatan lahā li-nabluwahum ayyuhum aḥsanu ʿamalan
versets.trad: What is upon the earth We have made an adornment for it, that We may test them as to which of them is best in deed;
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two adornments: one seen by the eyes, and one known by the secrets; the worshippers are the adornment of this world, the knowers the adornment of the Garden; the saints are the ornament of the earth and the safeguard of those who dwell on it. And as to “which is best in deed,” he rises: the best in deed is he who holds his own action most paltry, who most abases his obedience for the keenness with which he sees his own shortfall — for the fairest of a man's deeds is to look upon his deeds with the eye of contempt and belittlement.

mots.racine: ح س ن / ع م ل

versets.ar: وَإِنَّا لَجَاعِلُونَ مَا عَلَيْهَا صَعِيدًا جُرُزًا
versets.tr: wa-innā la-jāʿilūna mā ʿalayhā ṣaʿīdan juruzan
versets.trad: and We shall surely make what is upon it a barren plain.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the very fact that what is on the earth is now its adornment is stripped of all worth by the announcement that He will annihilate it in the end. What adorns the present is already doomed to become bare ground: the beauty of the world bears its own term within it.

versets.ar: أَمْ حَسِبْتَ أَنَّ أَصْحَابَ الْكَهْفِ وَالرَّقِيمِ كَانُوا مِنْ آيَاتِنَا عَجَبًا
versets.tr: am ḥasibta anna aṣḥāba l-kahfi wa-r-raqīmi kānū min āyātinā ʿajaban
versets.trad: Do you reckon that the Companions of the Cave and al-Raqīm were a wonder among Our signs?
themes: the sign that breaks the habitual
versets.commentaire: By referring their case to Himself — “among Our signs” — God removes all that is marvelous from their state: the overturning of the customary order, coming from God, is neither strange nor unprecedented. Qushayrī adds: souls have their places, hearts their dwellings, aspirations their free range — wherever one withdraws, one ever seeks his Companion. And he turns the sign toward the Prophet: do not wonder at their story, for your own state is more wondrous: in a fraction of a night you reached “two bows' length or nearer,” while they remained years in the cave.

mots.racine: ص ح ب / ك ه ف

mots.def: inscription, or name of the place/dog.

versets.ar: إِذْ أَوَى الْفِتْيَةُ إِلَى الْكَهْفِ فَقَالُوا رَبَّنَا آتِنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةً وَهَيِّئْ لَنَا مِنْ أَمْرِنَا رَشَدًا
versets.tr: idh awā l-fityatu ilā l-kahfi fa-qālū rabbanā ātinā min ladunka raḥmatan wa-hayyiʾ lanā min amrinā rashadan
versets.trad: When the young men took refuge in the cave, they cried: “Our Lord! Grant us a mercy from Yourself and dispose for us right guidance in our affair.”
themes: the refuge of the young men
versets.commentaire: He sheltered them in the cave as to their outward; but inwardly, says Qushayrī, it was in the shade of His turning toward them and His providence that they reposed; then He took them from themselves and undertook their charge, making the states run over them while they were absent from their own witnessing. Their opening prayer says all: they disowned their strength and power, and returned to God in the truth of their indigence; so He answered their plea, warded off their distress, and lodged them in the bosom of His refuge in a fair abode.

versets.ar: فَضَرَبْنَا عَلَىٰ آذَانِهِمْ فِي الْكَهْفِ سِنِينَ عَدَدًا
versets.tr: fa-ḍarabnā ʿalā ādhānihim fī l-kahfi sinīna ʿadadan
versets.trad: So We sealed their ears in the cave for a number of years.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads this sleep as a spiritual rapture: We withdrew them from the perception of their own selves, We snatched them from their own witnessing by immersing them in the realities We disclosed to them — the contemplation of Oneness and the permanence of the attribute of the Eternal Refuge (al-Ṣamad). The sleep of the bodies here figures the extinction (fanāʾ) of the knower absorbed in his Lord.

mots.racine: ض ر ب / أ ذ ن
mots.def: idiom: to plunge into deep sleep.

mots.racine: س ن و / ع د د

versets.ar: ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَاهُمْ لِنَعْلَمَ أَيُّ الْحِزْبَيْنِ أَحْصَىٰ لِمَا لَبِثُوا أَمَدًا
versets.tr: thumma baʿathnāhum li-naʿlama ayyu l-ḥizbayni aḥṣā li-mā labithū amadan
versets.trad: Then We raised them up, that We might know which of the two parties better reckoned the span of their stay.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We returned them to their state of lucidity and to the attributes of their discernment, We re-established them in the witnesses of separation (tafriqa) after We had effaced them from their witnessing by the attribute of union (jamʿ). The awakening is the return from union to distinction, from fanāʾ to subsistence.

versets.ar: نَّحْنُ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ نَبَأَهُم بِالْحَقِّ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ فِتْيَةٌ آمَنُوا بِرَبِّهِمْ وَزِدْنَاهُمْ هُدًى
versets.tr: naḥnu naquṣṣu ʿalayka nabaʾahum bi-l-ḥaqqi innahum fityatun āmanū bi-rabbihim wa-zidnāhum hudan
versets.trad: We relate to you their story in truth. They were young men who believed in their Lord, and We increased them in guidance.
versets.commentaire: Since they were taken from themselves, says Qushayrī, it was the Real who undertook to narrate in their stead; and he distinguishes one who can speak of himself, remaining in his witness, from one who, extinct to himself, can only be spoken of by Another. And he marvels: there is no telling of the beloved higher than what is heard from the Beloved Himself — “We relate to you”. These young men deserve the name fitya because they believed in their Lord without delay, the moment the calls of union reached them; they rose for God and did not halt until they reached God.

mots.def: qaṣṣa: to narrate, follow a track.

versets.ar: وَرَبَطْنَا عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ إِذْ قَامُوا فَقَالُوا رَبُّنَا رَبُّ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ لَن نَّدْعُوَ مِن دُونِهِ إِلَٰهًا ۖ لَّقَدْ قُلْنَا إِذًا شَطَطًا
versets.tr: wa-rabaṭnā ʿalā qulūbihim idh qāmū fa-qālū rabbunā rabbu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi lan nadʿuwa min dūnihi ilāhan laqad qulnā idhan shaṭaṭan
versets.trad: We fortified their hearts when they stood up and said: “Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. We shall never call upon any god besides Him, for then we would have uttered an abomination.
versets.commentaire: “We bound their hearts,” says Qushayrī, by the increase of certainty, until the full day of their knowings reached its zenith, the suns of their realization shone forth, and no room remained for hesitation in their thoughts. And further: We bound their hearts by extinguishing them to all others, dispensing them from all reflection through the lights of discernment We gave them, and settling within them the witnesses of the Unseen, so that no surmises of imagination nor whisperings of devils found access there. As to their standing: they stood up for God by God; whoever stands by God is absent to all but God.

mots.racine: ر ب ط / ق ل ب

versets.ar: هَٰؤُلَاءِ قَوْمُنَا اتَّخَذُوا مِن دُونِهِ آلِهَةً ۖ لَّوْلَا يَأْتُونَ عَلَيْهِم بِسُلْطَانٍ بَيِّنٍ ۖ فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرَىٰ عَلَى اللَّهِ كَذِبًا
versets.tr: hāʾulāʾi qawmunā ttakhadhū min dūnihi ālihatan lawlā yaʾtūna ʿalayhim bi-sulṭānin bayyinin fa-man aẓlamu mimmani ftarā ʿalā llāhi kadhiban
versets.trad: These, our people, have taken gods besides Him. Why do they not bring a clear authority for them? Who is more wrongdoing than he who forges a lie against God?
versets.commentaire: Having no proof, their claim was shown to be a lie, says Qushayrī: whoever contents himself with denying without bringing a witness to support his word is faulty in his creed. And he applies the verse to the Way: whoever states in religion a claim that no rational or transmitted proof upholds is a forger; and whoever lays claim to a state that neither the truth of his striving nor of his encounter justifies is a forger against God. The truthful one hears from the Real by his secret, then utters by his tongue only what he has received.

mots.racine: س ل ط / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَإِذِ اعْتَزَلْتُمُوهُمْ وَمَا يَعْبُدُونَ إِلَّا اللَّهَ فَأْوُوا إِلَى الْكَهْفِ يَنشُرْ لَكُمْ رَبُّكُم مِّن رَّحْمَتِهِ وَيُهَيِّئْ لَكُم مِّنْ أَمْرِكُم مِّرْفَقًا
versets.tr: wa-idhi ʿtazaltumūhum wa-mā yaʿbudūna illā llāha fa-ʾwū ilā l-kahfi yanshur lakum rabbukum min raḥmatihi wa-yuhayyiʾ lakum min amrikum mirfaqan
versets.trad: And when you have withdrawn from them and from what they worship besides God, take refuge in the cave; your Lord will spread His mercy over you and dispose for you ease in your affair.”
versets.commentaire: Here is a pearl of Qushayrī on withdrawal: withdrawing from what is not God necessitates union with God; rather, union with God is not attained save after withdrawal from what is not Him. And he unfolds the promise: when they withdrew from what was worshipped besides God, the Real sheltered them in the bosom of His safekeeping and prepared for them a lodging in the cave of His providence; whoever disowns his own contrivance, returns to God in truth in all his states, and seeks help from none but Him, him He receives in the bosom of His bounty, suffices in all his affairs, and prepares for him an abode shaded by the fullness of His turning toward him.

versets.ar: وَتَرَى الشَّمْسَ إِذَا طَلَعَت تَّزَاوَرُ عَن كَهْفِهِمْ ذَاتَ الْيَمِينِ وَإِذَا غَرَبَت تَّقْرِضُهُمْ ذَاتَ الشِّمَالِ وَهُمْ فِي فَجْوَةٍ مِّنْهُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ ۗ مَن يَهْدِ اللَّهُ فَهُوَ الْمُهْتَدِ ۖ وَمَن يُضْلِلْ فَلَن تَجِدَ لَهُ وَلِيًّا مُّرْشِدًا
versets.tr: wa-tarā sh-shamsa idhā ṭalaʿat tazāwaru ʿan kahfihim dhāta l-yamīni wa-idhā gharabat taqriḍuhum dhāta sh-shimāli wa-hum fī fajwatin minhu dhālika min āyāti llāhi man yahdi llāhu fa-huwa l-muhtadi wa-man yuḍlil fa-lan tajida lahu waliyyan murshidan
versets.trad: You would have seen the sun, as it rose, inclining away from their cave to the right, and as it set, passing them by to the left, while they lay in an open space within it. That is among the signs of God! Whom God guides is rightly guided, but whom He leads astray you will find for him no protecting guide.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī advances a luminous reading: the lights of the sun shrink and grow small beside their own lights; for the sun's light is a brightness by which creatures see, but the light of their knowings is that by which the Real is known — the one appears in the form, the other gleams in the inmost being. And on the two guidances: God guides one people by proofs and arguments, and another people by the unveiling of certainty; the first are people of demonstration, the second people of evidence, as though they were people of direct vision.

mots.def: tazāwara: to swerve to a side.
qaraḍa: to cut off, turn away.

versets.ar: وَتَحْسَبُهُمْ أَيْقَاظًا وَهُمْ رُقُودٌ ۚ وَنُقَلِّبُهُمْ ذَاتَ الْيَمِينِ وَذَاتَ الشِّمَالِ ۖ وَكَلْبُهُم بَاسِطٌ ذِرَاعَيْهِ بِالْوَصِيدِ ۚ لَوِ اطَّلَعْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَوَلَّيْتَ مِنْهُمْ فِرَارًا وَلَمُلِئْتَ مِنْهُمْ رُعْبًا
versets.tr: wa-taḥsabuhum ayqāẓan wa-hum ruqūdun wa-nuqallibuhum dhāta l-yamīni wa-dhāta sh-shimāli wa-kalbuhum bāsiṭun dhirāʿayhi bi-l-waṣīdi lawi ṭṭalaʿta ʿalayhim la-wallayta minhum firāran wa-la-muliʾta minhum ruʿban
versets.trad: You would have thought them awake, though they slept; and We turned them now to the right, now to the left, while their dog stretched out its forelegs on the threshold. Had you come upon them, you would have turned from them in flight, filled with dread at the sight of them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes this verse the emblem of the people of oneness: “you think them awake though they sleep” — by their outward they are under the witnesses of separation, but by the eye of union disclosed in their inmost being their states unfold without their applying themselves; they bear witness, extinct to what they do, that the One who acts in them is other than they. And “We turned them right and left”: tidings of the beauty of His refuge — not like the tenderness of mothers, but fuller; not like the mercy of fathers, but more august. As for the dog: whoever truly loves someone loves what attaches to him; a dog that walked a few steps with the beloved — until the Resurrection the Real says of it “their dog stretching out its forelegs.” A Muslim who keeps company with His saints from youth to old age — will He send him away disappointed on the Day of Judgment? No, He does not do so.

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ بَعَثْنَاهُمْ لِيَتَسَاءَلُوا بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ قَالَ قَائِلٌ مِّنْهُمْ كَمْ لَبِثْتُمْ ۖ قَالُوا لَبِثْنَا يَوْمًا أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ ۚ قَالُوا رَبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا لَبِثْتُمْ فَابْعَثُوا أَحَدَكُم بِوَرِقِكُمْ هَٰذِهِ إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ فَلْيَنظُرْ أَيُّهَا أَزْكَىٰ طَعَامًا فَلْيَأْتِكُم بِرِزْقٍ مِّنْهُ وَلْيَتَلَطَّفْ وَلَا يُشْعِرَنَّ بِكُمْ أَحَدًا
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika baʿathnāhum li-yatasāʾalū baynahum qāla qāʾilun minhum kam labithtum qālū labithnā yawman aw baʿḍa yawmin qālū rabbukum aʿlamu bi-mā labithtum fa-bʿathū aḥadakum bi-wariqikum hādhihi ilā l-madīnati fa-l-yanẓur ayyuhā azkā ṭaʿāman fa-l-yaʾtikum bi-rizqin minhu wa-l-yatalaṭṭaf wa-lā yushʿiranna bikum aḥadan
versets.trad: Thus We raised them up, that they might question one another. One of them said: “How long have you stayed?” Some said: “We have stayed a day, or part of a day.” Others said: “Your Lord knows best how long you have stayed. Send one of you with this coin of yours to the city; let him seek out the purest food and bring you provision of it. Let him be courteous and let no one become aware of you.
versets.commentaire: They thought their stay brief though it was long, says Qushayrī, because they were taken from themselves and had no knowledge of the detail of their states. And he finds in it the law of love: the days of union are short to them, however long; were the state reversed, the matter would be too — “your morning is intoxication and your evening intoxication again: blessed were you, but the days of joy are brief”. On the delicacy enjoined: they counseled one another to nobility of character and to courtesy; one who has attained knowledge is not content with the coarse in dress nor the common in food — nothing suits him but the refined.

mots.racine: ز ك و / ط ع م

versets.ar: إِنَّهُمْ إِن يَظْهَرُوا عَلَيْكُمْ يَرْجُمُوكُمْ أَوْ يُعِيدُوكُمْ فِي مِلَّتِهِمْ وَلَن تُفْلِحُوا إِذًا أَبَدًا
versets.tr: innahum in yaẓharū ʿalaykum yarjumūkum aw yuʿīdūkum fī millatihim wa-lan tufliḥū idhan abadan
versets.trad: For if they come upon you, they will stone you or force you back into their religion, and then you would never prosper.”
versets.commentaire: They counseled one another, says Qushayrī, to keep their secrets hidden from strangers, knowing that if discovered they would be forced either by killing or back to what they had freed themselves from. And he draws a law: the way of the pious is to guard their secrets from the alien; whoever reveals his secret to his enemies draws upon himself, by his own choice, his harm, and loses what gladdened him.

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَعْثَرْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ لِيَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌّ وَأَنَّ السَّاعَةَ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهَا إِذْ يَتَنَازَعُونَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَمْرَهُمْ ۖ فَقَالُوا ابْنُوا عَلَيْهِم بُنْيَانًا ۖ رَّبُّهُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِهِمْ ۚ قَالَ الَّذِينَ غَلَبُوا عَلَىٰ أَمْرِهِمْ لَنَتَّخِذَنَّ عَلَيْهِم مَّسْجِدًا
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika aʿtharnā ʿalayhim li-yaʿlamū anna waʿda llāhi ḥaqqun wa-anna s-sāʿata lā rayba fīhā idh yatanāzaʿūna baynahum amrahum fa-qālū bnū ʿalayhim bunyānan rabbuhum aʿlamu bihim qāla lladhīna ghalabū ʿalā amrihim la-nattakhidhanna ʿalayhim masjidan
versets.trad: Thus We made their case known, that the people might know that the promise of God is true and that there is no doubt about the Hour. When they disputed among themselves about their affair, some said: “Build over them a structure. Their Lord knows them best.” Those who prevailed in the matter said: “We shall surely raise over them a sanctuary.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He made their states a lesson for those who came after them, by disclosing their story to the people of the age; the people saw them with their eyes, and the certainty of those who believed in God increased when they beheld with their own sight what broke the constant custom. Then God returned them to their first state, taken from discernment, turned over in the Grip according to what the Real willed, deposited in what was disclosed to them, extinct to themselves in the existence of the Real.

versets.ar: سَيَقُولُونَ ثَلَاثَةٌ رَّابِعُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ وَيَقُولُونَ خَمْسَةٌ سَادِسُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ رَجْمًا بِالْغَيْبِ ۖ وَيَقُولُونَ سَبْعَةٌ وَثَامِنُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ ۚ قُل رَّبِّي أَعْلَمُ بِعِدَّتِهِم مَّا يَعْلَمُهُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌ ۗ فَلَا تُمَارِ فِيهِمْ إِلَّا مِرَاءً ظَاهِرًا وَلَا تَسْتَفْتِ فِيهِم مِّنْهُمْ أَحَدًا
versets.tr: sa-yaqūlūna thalāthatun rābiʿuhum kalbuhum wa-yaqūlūna khamsatun sādisuhum kalbuhum rajman bi-l-ghaybi wa-yaqūlūna sabʿatun wa-thāminuhum kalbuhum qul rabbī aʿlamu bi-ʿiddatihim mā yaʿlamuhum illā qalīlun fa-lā tumāri fīhim illā mirāʾan ẓāhiran wa-lā tastafti fīhim minhum aḥadan
versets.trad: Some will say: “Three, their dog the fourth.” Others will say: “Five, their dog the sixth,” guessing at the unseen. And others will say: “Seven, their dog the eighth.” Say: “My Lord knows best their number; only a few know them.” So do not dispute about them save in an outward dispute, and do not seek the opinion of any of them concerning them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the knowledge of people falls short of their number; how should creatures have access to the states that God alone knows in the secrets and hearts? And he makes of it a law of sainthood: because they were among His saints, only His elect servants can know them; the Real veils His saints from strangers, so that only the people of the Reality recognize them — strangers do not know the near ones, but the near ones are never a riddle to the near ones. Thus the masters of this community said: “The Sufis are the people of one household; none other enters among them.”

mots.racine: ر ج م / غ ي ب

versets.ar: وَلَا تَقُولَنَّ لِشَيْءٍ إِنِّي فَاعِلٌ ذَٰلِكَ غَدًا
versets.tr: wa-lā taqūlanna li-shayʾin innī fāʿilun dhālika ghadan
versets.trad: And never say of anything: “I shall surely do that tomorrow,”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: since events proceed from God's will, whoever knows God no longer counts from himself what he knows is accomplished only by God; whoever knows God sees his own choice fall away before the divine will, and his judgments dissolve into his contemplation of God's decree. The believer resolves in his heart to embrace future obedience, while disowning, by his secret, his own power.

versets.ar: إِلَّا أَن يَشَاءَ اللَّهُ ۚ وَاذْكُر رَّبَّكَ إِذَا نَسِيتَ وَقُلْ عَسَىٰ أَن يَهْدِيَنِ رَبِّي لِأَقْرَبَ مِنْ هَٰذَا رَشَدًا
versets.tr: illā an yashāʾa llāhu wa-dhkur rabbaka idhā nasīta wa-qul ʿasā an yahdiyani rabbī li-aqraba min hādhā rashadan
versets.trad: unless [you add] “If God wills.” And remember your Lord when you forget, and say: “May my Lord guide me to what is nearer than this in rectitude.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds several senses of “remember your Lord when you forget”: if forgetfulness befalls you without your fault, strip your intention by your remembrance from the dwellings of heedlessness; or remember your Lord when you forget your own remembrance of Him — for the servant who watches his own dhikr spoils its purity; or again remember your Lord when you forget your portion of Him; remember Him when you forget all other than Him.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ر ب ب

versets.ar: وَلَبِثُوا فِي كَهْفِهِمْ ثَلَاثَ مِائَةٍ سِنِينَ وَازْدَادُوا تِسْعًا
versets.tr: wa-labithū fī kahfihim thalātha miʾatin sinīna wa-zdādū tisʿan
versets.trad: They stayed in their cave three hundred years, and nine were added.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they were taken from themselves in the perception of their being, and so had no awareness of the length of their span. And he cites the adage: “the days of joy are short” — ages, in gladness, are months, and months, in trial, are ages. The measure of time follows the state of the heart, not the clock.

mots.racine: ث ل ث / م أ ي
ز ي د / ت س ع

versets.ar: قُلِ اللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا لَبِثُوا ۖ لَهُ غَيْبُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۖ أَبْصِرْ بِهِ وَأَسْمِعْ ۚ مَا لَهُم مِّن دُونِهِ مِن وَلِيٍّ وَلَا يُشْرِكُ فِي حُكْمِهِ أَحَدًا
versets.tr: quli llāhu aʿlamu bi-mā labithū lahu ghaybu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi abṣir bihi wa-asmiʿ mā lahum min dūnihi min waliyyin wa-lā yushriku fī ḥukmihi aḥadan
versets.trad: Say: “God knows best how long they stayed. To Him belongs the unseen of the heavens and the earth. How well He sees and hears! They have no protector besides Him, and He associates none in His judgment.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever no longer counts his days because he is occupied with God, God counts for him his breaths given to God — as in the saying: “He has numbered all things in number.” The servant absorbed in his Lord loses the measure of time, but the Lord keeps its exact count.

mots.racine: غ ي ب / س م و
ب ص ر / س م ع

versets.ar: وَاتْلُ مَا أُوحِيَ إِلَيْكَ مِن كِتَابِ رَبِّكَ ۖ لَا مُبَدِّلَ لِكَلِمَاتِهِ وَلَن تَجِدَ مِن دُونِهِ مُلْتَحَدًا
versets.tr: wa-tlu mā ūḥiya ilayka min kitābi rabbika lā mubaddila li-kalimātihi wa-lan tajida min dūnihi multaḥadan
versets.trad: Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book of your Lord; none can change His words, and you will find no refuge besides Him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: console yourself, when the states vary upon you, with what We disclose to you of the tidings; for the letters of the beloved bear healing, being the address of the beloved to the beloved. And on “none changes His words”: no altering of His decree — whom He distances finds no acceptance, whom He draws near finds no estrangement; whom He accepts knows no rejection, whom He establishes knows no barring.

versets.ar: وَاصْبِرْ نَفْسَكَ مَعَ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُم بِالْغَدَاةِ وَالْعَشِيِّ يُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَهُ ۖ وَلَا تَعْدُ عَيْنَاكَ عَنْهُمْ تُرِيدُ زِينَةَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَا تُطِعْ مَنْ أَغْفَلْنَا قَلْبَهُ عَن ذِكْرِنَا وَاتَّبَعَ هَوَاهُ وَكَانَ أَمْرُهُ فُرُطًا
versets.tr: wa-ṣbir nafsaka maʿa lladhīna yadʿūna rabbahum bi-l-ghadāti wa-l-ʿashiyyi yurīdūna wajhahu wa-lā taʿdu ʿaynāka ʿanhum turīdu zīnata l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-lā tuṭiʿ man aghfalnā qalbahu ʿan dhikrinā wa-ttabaʿa hawāhu wa-kāna amruhu furuṭan
versets.trad: Keep yourself patient with those who call upon their Lord morning and evening, desiring His Face. Let not your eyes pass beyond them, desiring the adornment of the life of this world. And do not obey one whose heart We have made heedless of Our remembrance, who follows his caprice and whose conduct is unbridled.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the subtlety: He says “keep your soul patient” and not “your heart,” because his heart was with the Real; He commands him openly to keep his soul upright, and reserves his heart in secret for Himself. And he reveals the verse's hidden tenderness: when they looked toward God with their hearts, He commanded His Messenger not to turn his gaze from them — this is their reward here below. The allusion is: We have made your gaze upon them today a passage for them to Us, in compensation for what they lose by not seeing Us now; so do not withdraw your gaze from them, for tomorrow We shall not deny them their gaze upon Us.

mots.racine: ص ب ر / ن ف س
ر و د / و ج ه

versets.ar: وَقُلِ الْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ فَمَن شَاءَ فَلْيُؤْمِن وَمَن شَاءَ فَلْيَكْفُرْ ۚ إِنَّا أَعْتَدْنَا لِلظَّالِمِينَ نَارًا أَحَاطَ بِهِمْ سُرَادِقُهَا ۚ وَإِن يَسْتَغِيثُوا يُغَاثُوا بِمَاءٍ كَالْمُهْلِ يَشْوِي الْوُجُوهَ ۚ بِئْسَ الشَّرَابُ وَسَاءَتْ مُرْتَفَقًا
versets.tr: wa-quli l-ḥaqqu min rabbikum fa-man shāʾa fa-l-yuʾmin wa-man shāʾa fa-l-yakfur innā aʿtadnā li-ẓ-ẓālimīna nāran aḥāṭa bihim surādiquhā wa-in yastaghīthū yughāthū bi-māʾin ka-l-muhli yashwī l-wujūha biʾsa sh-sharābu wa-sāʾat murtafaqan
versets.trad: Say: “The Truth is from your Lord. Let whoever wills believe, and whoever wills disbelieve.” Indeed We have prepared for the wrongdoers a Fire whose walls will encompass them. If they cry for help, they will be helped with water like molten metal that scalds the faces. Wretched the drink, and evil the resting-place!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: say, O Muḥammad: what comes to you from your Lord is truth; let whoever wills believe, and whoever wills disbelieve — the utmost of warning. If you believe, the fruits of your faith return to you alone; if you refuse, the chastisement of denial rests on you alone. For the Real is Mighty: no ornament accrues to Him from the faith of all if they affirm His oneness, no blemish from the unbelief of all if they deny Him.

mots.def: muhl: dregs of oil, molten metal.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ إِنَّا لَا نُضِيعُ أَجْرَ مَنْ أَحْسَنَ عَمَلًا
versets.tr: inna lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti innā lā nuḍīʿu ajra man aḥsana ʿamalan
versets.trad: As for those who believe and do righteous deeds — We do not let the reward of one who does good go to waste.
versets.commentaire: Here is a pearl of Qushayrī: whoever receives upon himself a speck of dust on Our road never receives the darkness of Our separation; whoever takes a step toward Us finds favor with Us; whoever advances a foot toward Us, We forgive him what he has already done; whoever raises a hand to Us, We lavish abundance upon him; whoever takes refuge at the threshold of Our generosity, We shelter in the shade of Our graces. And the fair deed is that whose doer is absent from seeing it, who strips his intention of all portion and share.

mots.sens: We do not let go to waste

mots.racine: ح س ن / ع م ل

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهِمُ الْأَنْهَارُ يُحَلَّوْنَ فِيهَا مِنْ أَسَاوِرَ مِن ذَهَبٍ وَيَلْبَسُونَ ثِيَابًا خُضْرًا مِّن سُندُسٍ وَإِسْتَبْرَقٍ مُّتَّكِئِينَ فِيهَا عَلَى الْأَرَائِكِ ۚ نِعْمَ الثَّوَابُ وَحَسُنَتْ مُرْتَفَقًا
versets.tr: ulāʾika lahum jannātu ʿadnin tajrī min taḥtihimu l-anhāru yuḥallawna fīhā min asāwira min dhahabin wa-yalbasūna thiyāban khuḍran min sundusin wa-istabraqin muttakiʾīna fīhā ʿalā l-arāʾiki niʿma th-thawābu wa-ḥasunat murtafaqan
versets.trad: Theirs are the Gardens of Eden beneath which rivers flow; they will be adorned therein with bracelets of gold and clothed in green garments of fine silk and brocade, reclining upon couches. Excellent the reward, and fair the resting-place!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī raises the bliss of the Garden to that of nearness: they are the people of the gardens, in the sweetness of living and the felicity of fortune; they don the robes of union and are crowned with the diadem of nearness, borne upon the carpets, reclining on the couches, breathing the perfumes of intimacy, dwelling in the arena of proximity, given to drink the wine of love; and the Real gives them to drink a pure draught that washes their hearts of the love of every creature. “Excellent the reward”: excellent their wage, excellent their Lord, excellent their abode, excellent their Neighbor, excellent their state.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ع د ن
س و ر / ذ ه ب
س ن د س / ب ر ق
mots.def: sundus: light silk; istabraq: heavy brocade.

versets.ar: وَاضْرِبْ لَهُم مَّثَلًا رَّجُلَيْنِ جَعَلْنَا لِأَحَدِهِمَا جَنَّتَيْنِ مِنْ أَعْنَابٍ وَحَفَفْنَاهُمَا بِنَخْلٍ وَجَعَلْنَا بَيْنَهُمَا زَرْعًا
versets.tr: wa-ḍrib lahum mathalan rajulayni jaʿalnā li-aḥadihimā jannatayni min aʿnābin wa-ḥafafnāhumā bi-nakhlin wa-jaʿalnā baynahumā zarʿan
versets.trad: Set forth for them the parable of two men: to one of them We gave two gardens of grapevines, which We encircled with palms and set between them tilled fields.
themes: the parable of the two men
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He created two men and gave them two gardens; one gave thanks to his Creator, the other denied his Provider. And he transposes it into the Way: He creates two servants, makes the moment sweet for them, spreads out the carpet of kindness; one rises toward the end of the stations by the beauty of his halt and the truth of his dealing, until he is rapt by the realities of Oneness; the other does not measure the worth of the fair beginning granted him, returns to his habits, and falls into the darkness of heedlessness.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ع ن ب
ح ف ف / ن خ ل

versets.ar: كِلْتَا الْجَنَّتَيْنِ آتَتْ أُكُلَهَا وَلَمْ تَظْلِم مِّنْهُ شَيْئًا ۚ وَفَجَّرْنَا خِلَالَهُمَا نَهَرًا
versets.tr: kiltā l-jannatayni ātat ukulahā wa-lam taẓlim minhu shayʾan wa-fajjarnā khilālahumā naharan
versets.trad: Both gardens yielded their produce, withholding nothing of it, and We made a river gush forth between them.
versets.commentaire: The perfect gift of the two gardens, in Qushayrī's reading, is precisely the trial: the grace spread out tests the servant. He who sees the Bestower in the gift rises; he who stops at the gift and forgets the Giver prepares his own fall. Abundance that lacks nothing is a threshold: either it leads to gratitude and union, or it seduces and leads astray.

versets.ar: وَكَانَ لَهُ ثَمَرٌ فَقَالَ لِصَاحِبِهِ وَهُوَ يُحَاوِرُهُ أَنَا أَكْثَرُ مِنكَ مَالًا وَأَعَزُّ نَفَرًا
versets.tr: wa-kāna lahu thamarun fa-qāla li-ṣāḥibihi wa-huwa yuḥāwiruhu anā aktharu minka mālan wa-aʿazzu nafaran
versets.trad: He had fruit, and he said to his companion, conversing with him: “I am greater than you in wealth and mightier in following!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he who prides himself on his wealth and his clan forgets his Master in the hour of heedlessness. Whoever leans on his possessions, is seduced by his children, and forgets his Master in the moments of his negligence, loses in his state and repents of what he has missed at his end. The pride of wealth is the first veil that separates from the Giver.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ن ف ر

versets.ar: وَدَخَلَ جَنَّتَهُ وَهُوَ ظَالِمٌ لِّنَفْسِهِ قَالَ مَا أَظُنُّ أَن تَبِيدَ هَٰذِهِ أَبَدًا
versets.tr: wa-dakhala jannatahu wa-huwa ẓālimun li-nafsihi qāla mā aẓunnu an tabīda hādhihi abadan
versets.trad: And he entered his garden, wronging himself, and said: “I do not think this will ever perish.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the proud man imagines his wealth eternal because he has severed it from the hand of the One who gives and takes. To believe grace permanent in itself, apart from the will of the Giver, is to wrong oneself: for nothing subsists by itself, and the creature that claims duration blinds itself to its own indigence.

mots.racine: د خ ل / ج ن ن
ظ ل م / ن ف س

versets.ar: وَمَا أَظُنُّ السَّاعَةَ قَائِمَةً وَلَئِن رُّدِدتُّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّي لَأَجِدَنَّ خَيْرًا مِّنْهَا مُنقَلَبًا
versets.tr: wa-mā aẓunnu s-sāʿata qāʾimatan wa-laʾin rudidtu ilā rabbī la-ajidanna khayran minhā munqalaban
versets.trad: Nor do I think the Hour will come; and even if I am returned to my Lord, I shall surely find a better resort than this.”
versets.commentaire: The twofold denial — of the Hour and of dependence — is, for Qushayrī, the height of presumption: not content with believing his wealth eternal, the man claims that even if returned to God he would find something better still. He measures the divine generosity by his own supposed merit, and makes of his present privilege the guarantee of a future one — ignorant that grace is neither earned nor demanded.

mots.racine: س و ع / ق و م

versets.ar: قَالَ لَهُ صَاحِبُهُ وَهُوَ يُحَاوِرُهُ أَكَفَرْتَ بِالَّذِي خَلَقَكَ مِن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ ثُمَّ سَوَّاكَ رَجُلًا
versets.tr: qāla lahu ṣāḥibuhu wa-huwa yuḥāwiruhu a-kafarta bi-lladhī khalaqaka min turābin thumma min nuṭfatin thumma sawwāka rajulan
versets.trad: His companion, conversing with him, said: “Do you disbelieve in the One who created you from dust, then from a drop, then fashioned you a man?
versets.commentaire: The believing companion recalls the humble origin to break the pride: dust, a drop, then the form of a man. Qushayrī sees in this reminder true wisdom: to contemplate one's beginning from nothing dispels the illusion of self-sufficiency. Whoever remembers whence he came no longer thinks himself the source of what he possesses.

mots.def: kafara: to be ungrateful, to disbelieve.

mots.racine: س و ي / ر ج ل

versets.ar: لَّٰكِنَّا هُوَ اللَّهُ رَبِّي وَلَا أُشْرِكُ بِرَبِّي أَحَدًا
versets.tr: lākinnā huwa llāhu rabbī wa-lā ushriku bi-rabbī aḥadan
versets.trad: But as for me, He is God, my Lord, and I associate none with my Lord.
versets.commentaire: In the face of ingratitude, the believer proclaims his pure fidelity: “He, God, is my Lord, and I associate none with Him.” Qushayrī makes of this word the summit of the station: to refer neither one's wealth nor one's poverty to other than God is the truth of oneness (tawḥīd) lived, where the servant acknowledges no agent but his Lord.

versets.ar: وَلَوْلَا إِذْ دَخَلْتَ جَنَّتَكَ قُلْتَ مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ لَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ ۚ إِن تَرَنِ أَنَا أَقَلَّ مِنكَ مَالًا وَوَلَدًا
versets.tr: wa-lawlā idh dakhalta jannataka qulta mā shāʾa llāhu lā quwwata illā bi-llāhi in tarani anā aqalla minka mālan wa-waladan
versets.trad: Why, when you entered your garden, did you not say: ‘What God wills! There is no power save in God!’ If you see me less than you in wealth and children,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes “What God wills, there is no power save in God” the word that would have saved the garden: to refer every gift to the divine will and every power to God alone is to protect grace by binding it to its Source. Whoever, on entering his wealth, ascribes it to God's will and disowns his own power, shelters his gift from annihilation.

versets.ar: فَعَسَىٰ رَبِّي أَن يُؤْتِيَنِ خَيْرًا مِّن جَنَّتِكَ وَيُرْسِلَ عَلَيْهَا حُسْبَانًا مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ فَتُصْبِحَ صَعِيدًا زَلَقًا
versets.tr: fa-ʿasā rabbī an yuʾtiyani khayran min jannatika wa-yursila ʿalayhā ḥusbānan mina s-samāʾi fa-tuṣbiḥa ṣaʿīdan zalaqan
versets.trad: it may be that my Lord will give me better than your garden and send upon it a thunderbolt from heaven, so that it becomes a slippery, barren slope;
versets.commentaire: The believer turns the proud man's own logic against him: what God gives, He can take back, and the garden the other thought eternal can in an instant become slippery, bare ground. Qushayrī reads in it the law of the decree: grace ascribed to oneself is fragile, but grace entrusted to God endures; the hope of the poor in God is worth more than the assurance of the rich in his wealth.

mots.racine: ع س ي / ر ب ب
mots.sens: it may be that my Lord

mots.racine: ص ع د / ز ل ق

versets.ar: أَوْ يُصْبِحَ مَاؤُهَا غَوْرًا فَلَن تَسْتَطِيعَ لَهُ طَلَبًا
versets.tr: aw yuṣbiḥa māʾuhā ghawran fa-lan tastaṭīʿa lahu ṭalaban
versets.trad: or its water sinks deep into the earth so that you can never reach it.”
versets.commentaire: The engulfed, irrecoverable water figures, for Qushayrī, grace withdrawn that no effort brings back: when God withdraws the secret of life, no stratagem restores it. The man who thought he mastered his spring discovers his utter powerlessness: provision comes from God and returns to God, and the creature has no hold upon it.

mots.def: ghawr: water gone beneath the earth.

versets.ar: وَأُحِيطَ بِثَمَرِهِ فَأَصْبَحَ يُقَلِّبُ كَفَّيْهِ عَلَىٰ مَا أَنفَقَ فِيهَا وَهِيَ خَاوِيَةٌ عَلَىٰ عُرُوشِهَا وَيَقُولُ يَا لَيْتَنِي لَمْ أُشْرِكْ بِرَبِّي أَحَدًا
versets.tr: wa-uḥīṭa bi-thamarihi fa-aṣbaḥa yuqallibu kaffayhi ʿalā mā anfaqa fīhā wa-hiya khāwiyatun ʿalā ʿurūshihā wa-yaqūlu yā laytanī lam ushrik bi-rabbī aḥadan
versets.trad: His fruit was encompassed by ruin, and he began to wring his hands over what he had spent on it, as it lay fallen upon its trellises, saying: “Would that I had associated none with my Lord!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the loss of one who preferred his own portion to God's right breaks upon him, he knocks at the door of repentance — but it avails him nothing. And he contrasts the two times: had he knocked at the door of His generosity in this world, at the hour of his lapse, He would have aided him in his distress and saved him from his ruin; but he was bound to abandonment, and the matter was veiled from him by the law of gradual seduction (istidrāj).

mots.racine: ح و ط / ث م ر
ق ل ب / ك ف ف
خ و ي / ع ر ش

versets.ar: وَلَمْ تَكُن لَّهُ فِئَةٌ يَنصُرُونَهُ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَمَا كَانَ مُنتَصِرًا
versets.tr: wa-lam takun lahu fiʾatun yanṣurūnahu min dūni llāhi wa-mā kāna muntaṣiran
versets.trad: He had no host to help him against God, nor could he help himself.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: one whose affair becomes notorious by the sovereign's anger upon him — none of the soldiers nor subjects looks at him any longer; likewise one whom the Real marks with the brand of abandonment, no king nor prophet pities him, no friend nor saint protects him. The ingrate finds himself alone, for he has severed the one bond that saves.

versets.ar: هُنَالِكَ الْوَلَايَةُ لِلَّهِ الْحَقِّ ۚ هُوَ خَيْرٌ ثَوَابًا وَخَيْرٌ عُقْبًا
versets.tr: hunālika l-walāyatu li-llāhi l-ḥaqqi huwa khayrun thawāban wa-khayrun ʿuqban
versets.trad: There, protection belongs to God, the Truth; He is best in reward and best in outcome.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He is the Real, alone bearing the attribute of His sovereignty; He associates no contingent being in the majesty of His power. When a spark of the sovereignty of the Reality shines forth, no claim nor meaning holds for man, no weight nor worth for the contingent — nay! He is God, the Creator, the One, the Subduer. Walāya belongs to God alone: by Him power, by Him help.

versets.ar: وَاضْرِبْ لَهُم مَّثَلَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا كَمَاءٍ أَنزَلْنَاهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ فَاخْتَلَطَ بِهِ نَبَاتُ الْأَرْضِ فَأَصْبَحَ هَشِيمًا تَذْرُوهُ الرِّيَاحُ ۗ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُّقْتَدِرًا
versets.tr: wa-ḍrib lahum mathala l-ḥayāti d-dunyā ka-māʾin anzalnāhu mina s-samāʾi fa-khtalaṭa bihi nabātu l-arḍi fa-aṣbaḥa hashīman tadhrūhu r-riyāḥu wa-kāna llāhu ʿalā kulli shayʾin muqtadiran
versets.trad: Set forth for them the parable of the life of this world: it is like water We send down from the sky; the plants of the earth absorb it, then they become dry stalks scattered by the winds. God has power over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives a bitter pearl on the world: whoever settles his soul on this world and its glitter, the world deceives him with its promises and dupes him with its cravings; then it hides the bitter aloe in its drink, the colocynth in its honey, the mirage in its aims. It promises and does not keep, its calamities outweigh its bounties, its graces are mixed with afflictions, its joy escorted by misery. The deceived is he who is taken in by it, the cheated he who is seduced by it.

mots.racine: ذ ر و / ر و ح

versets.ar: الْمَالُ وَالْبَنُونَ زِينَةُ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا ۖ وَالْبَاقِيَاتُ الصَّالِحَاتُ خَيْرٌ عِندَ رَبِّكَ ثَوَابًا وَخَيْرٌ أَمَلًا
versets.tr: al-mālu wa-l-banūna zīnatu l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-l-bāqiyātu ṣ-ṣāliḥātu khayrun ʿinda rabbika thawāban wa-khayrun amalan
versets.trad: Wealth and children are the adornment of the life of this world. But the enduring good deeds are better in reward with your Lord, and better in hope.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two adornments: the adornment of the heedless in this world is in wealth and children; the adornment of the people of union is in deeds and certainty — the former have their ornament in their outward, the latter in their servanthood and their pride in knowing the lordship of their Lord. And the “enduring deeds” are, for him, what is pure for God alone, free of craving and of aim; what gleams in the secrets when the servant is adorned with the attributes of nearness, and whose fragrance rises to the heaven of the Kingdom.

mots.racine: م و ل / ب ن ي
ز ي ن / ح ي و
ب ق ي / ص ل ح

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ نُسَيِّرُ الْجِبَالَ وَتَرَى الْأَرْضَ بَارِزَةً وَحَشَرْنَاهُمْ فَلَمْ نُغَادِرْ مِنْهُمْ أَحَدًا
versets.tr: wa-yawma nusayyiru l-jibāla wa-tarā l-arḍa bārizatan wa-ḥasharnāhum fa-lam nughādir minhum aḥadan
versets.trad: On the Day We shall set the mountains in motion, you will see the earth laid bare as a plain, and We shall gather them, leaving not one of them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives a striking reading of the mountains: just as the mountains of the earth will be set in motion on the Day of Resurrection, they are uprooted by the death of the Substitutes (abdāl) by whom the Real today maintains the earth — for these lords are in truth the pillars of the world. And “We left not one of them”: there is none who does not drink the cup of death; their nobility, in the degrees, lies in their withdrawal from cohabitation with the world.

mots.racine: س ي ر / ج ب ل
mots.sens: We set the mountains in motion

versets.ar: وَعُرِضُوا عَلَىٰ رَبِّكَ صَفًّا لَّقَدْ جِئْتُمُونَا كَمَا خَلَقْنَاكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ۚ بَلْ زَعَمْتُمْ أَلَّن نَّجْعَلَ لَكُم مَّوْعِدًا
versets.tr: wa-ʿuriḍū ʿalā rabbika ṣaffan laqad jiʾtumūnā kamā khalaqnākum awwala marratin bal zaʿamtum allan najʿala lakum mawʿidan
versets.trad: They will be presented before your Lord in ranks: “You have come to Us as We created you the first time. Yet you claimed that We would set no appointment for you!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the diversity of the day of presentation: He makes each one stand, on the day of presentation, in a particular witness, and clothes each with what befits him — one with the garment of piety, one with the shirt of passion, one with the sash of ecstasy, one with the cuirass of love, one with the cloak of yearning, one with the robe of union. To some He says: “Peace be upon you, how were you, how you longed for Us!”; to others: “What did you do, what did you squander?”

versets.ar: وَوُضِعَ الْكِتَابُ فَتَرَى الْمُجْرِمِينَ مُشْفِقِينَ مِمَّا فِيهِ وَيَقُولُونَ يَا وَيْلَتَنَا مَالِ هَٰذَا الْكِتَابِ لَا يُغَادِرُ صَغِيرَةً وَلَا كَبِيرَةً إِلَّا أَحْصَاهَا ۚ وَوَجَدُوا مَا عَمِلُوا حَاضِرًا ۗ وَلَا يَظْلِمُ رَبُّكَ أَحَدًا
versets.tr: wa-wuḍiʿa l-kitābu fa-tarā l-mujrimīna mushfiqīna mimmā fīhi wa-yaqūlūna yā waylatanā mā li-hādhā l-kitābi lā yughādiru ṣaghīratan wa-lā kabīratan illā aḥṣāhā wa-wajadū mā ʿamilū ḥāḍiran wa-lā yaẓlimu rabbuka aḥadan
versets.trad: The Book will be set down, and you will see the guilty in dread of what is in it, saying: “Woe to us! What kind of Book is this that leaves nothing, small or great, but it has counted it?” They will find present before them all they did, and your Lord wrongs no one.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts two dreads: the heart of the sincere is filled with sorrow, for if he sees a fault in his deed, he is in a place of shame for his shortfall; and if he sees a good deed, he is still in a place of shame for the little reverence he put into it — so that the shame of the truthful before their good deeds surpasses that of the heedless on discovering their faults. And he adds: on the day of reckoning, the fault, though committed years ago, will appear to them as though they had never left it.

mots.racine: و ض ع / ك ت ب

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ اسْجُدُوا لِآدَمَ فَسَجَدُوا إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ كَانَ مِنَ الْجِنِّ فَفَسَقَ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِ ۗ أَفَتَتَّخِذُونَهُ وَذُرِّيَّتَهُ أَوْلِيَاءَ مِن دُونِي وَهُمْ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ ۚ بِئْسَ لِلظَّالِمِينَ بَدَلًا
versets.tr: wa-idh qulnā li-l-malāʾikati sjudū li-ādama fa-sajadū illā iblīsa kāna mina l-jinni fa-fasaqa ʿan amri rabbihi a-fa-tattakhidhūnahu wa-dhurriyyatahu awliyāʾa min dūnī wa-hum lakum ʿaduwwun biʾsa li-ẓ-ẓālimīna badalan
versets.trad: When We said to the angels: “Prostrate before Adam!” they prostrated, except Iblīs, who was of the jinn and rebelled against the command of his Lord. Will you take him and his offspring as protectors besides Me, when they are enemies to you? Evil is the exchange for the wrongdoers!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He manifested to the angels a spark of that by which He had chosen Adam, and they prostrated by a facilitation from God; but He made the accursed one's sight drunk, so that he saw only the eye of matter — Iblīs looked at the clay body and said “You created me from fire,” without seeing the hidden jewel; and his word “I am better than him” was a lie once he departed from the command. The verse teaches that whoever devotes his fidelity to God alone follows no other and fears no other.

versets.ar: مَّا أَشْهَدتُّهُمْ خَلْقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَلَا خَلْقَ أَنفُسِهِمْ وَمَا كُنتُ مُتَّخِذَ الْمُضِلِّينَ عَضُدًا
versets.tr: mā ashhadtuhum khalqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-lā khalqa anfusihim wa-mā kuntu muttakhidha l-muḍillīna ʿaḍudan
versets.trad: I did not make them witness the creation of the heavens and the earth, nor their own creation, nor did I take the misleaders as helpers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if the knowledge of creatures falls short of their own knowledge of themselves, how should it encompass the realities of the Eternal Refuge? And he draws the right orientation: let them turn their effort toward the knowledge of God, of His attributes and His decrees, for religion makes this a duty upon them — the servant must know his Worshipped with a knowledge that removes doubt from his heart.

mots.sens: I did not make them witness

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ن ف س
ض ل ل / ع ض د

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ يَقُولُ نَادُوا شُرَكَائِيَ الَّذِينَ زَعَمْتُمْ فَدَعَوْهُمْ فَلَمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا لَهُمْ وَجَعَلْنَا بَيْنَهُم مَّوْبِقًا
versets.tr: wa-yawma yaqūlu nādū shurakāʾiya lladhīna zaʿamtum fa-daʿawhum fa-lam yastajībū lahum wa-jaʿalnā baynahum mawbiqan
versets.trad: On the Day He says: “Call upon those you claimed to be My partners,” they will call them, but they will not answer them, for We shall set between them a place of perdition.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Real knows that the idols can neither profit nor harm, but He will make this known to them at the end by a knowledge that becomes necessary for them, cutting off their illusions — they who imagined that their worship of idols brought them near to God, saying “we worship them only that they may bring us nearer to God.” When this truth is forced upon them, their regret will be total, and that is the harshest of chastisements.

mots.racine: ن د و / ش ر ك

versets.ar: وَرَأَى الْمُجْرِمُونَ النَّارَ فَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُم مُّوَاقِعُوهَا وَلَمْ يَجِدُوا عَنْهَا مَصْرِفًا
versets.tr: wa-raʾā l-mujrimūna n-nāra fa-ẓannū annahum muwāqiʿūhā wa-lam yajidū ʿanhā maṣrifan
versets.trad: The guilty will see the Fire and be certain they are to fall into it, and they will find no escape from it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the illusions are cut off, the knowledges become necessary and the Fire is seen with their own eyes, they will be certain they are to fall into it; then no excuse will be heard, no stratagem will avail, no intercession accepted, no ransom or substitute received. Disappointment will be total, despair will prevail, hopelessness will settle in — and that is the greatest of chastisements.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ صَرَّفْنَا فِي هَٰذَا الْقُرْآنِ لِلنَّاسِ مِن كُلِّ مَثَلٍ ۚ وَكَانَ الْإِنسَانُ أَكْثَرَ شَيْءٍ جَدَلًا
versets.tr: wa-laqad ṣarrafnā fī hādhā l-qurʾāni li-n-nāsi min kulli mathalin wa-kāna l-insānu akthara shayʾin jadalan
versets.trad: We have set forth for people in this Qurʾān every kind of parable, yet man is the most contentious of beings.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He made the proofs clear for all, but He confounded the way for some, and they fell into the twisting. And he distinguishes two disputes: dispute for God, against His enemies, is praiseworthy; but dispute with God is association, for it amounts to opposing while imagining that anyone could contest the decree. Among the signs of the believer's felicity: that the door of action be opened to him and the door of dispute closed against him.

mots.def: ṣarrafa: to set out in diverse forms.

mots.racine: ك ث ر / ج د ل

versets.ar: وَمَا مَنَعَ النَّاسَ أَن يُؤْمِنُوا إِذْ جَاءَهُمُ الْهُدَىٰ وَيَسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّهُمْ إِلَّا أَن تَأْتِيَهُمْ سُنَّةُ الْأَوَّلِينَ أَوْ يَأْتِيَهُمُ الْعَذَابُ قُبُلًا
versets.tr: wa-mā manaʿa n-nāsa an yuʾminū idh jāʾahumu l-hudā wa-yastaghfirū rabbahum illā an taʾtiyahum sunnatu l-awwalīna aw yaʾtiyahumu l-ʿadhābu qubulan
versets.trad: Nothing prevented people from believing when guidance came to them, and from seeking forgiveness of their Lord, except that the way of the ancients should befall them, or that the chastisement should come upon them face to face.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they have no excuse in taking refuge in disobedience and the refusal to hasten to the command; no divine assistance supports them to carry them from the call to the resolve to act. He describes here the state of takhliya (relinquishment): they are neither endowed with the capacity to do what they do not do, nor incapable of it — it is an intermediate state between power and incapacity, in which a man, were he to will, would find himself barred.

mots.racine: س ن ن / أ و ل

versets.ar: وَمَا نُرْسِلُ الْمُرْسَلِينَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرِينَ وَمُنذِرِينَ ۚ وَيُجَادِلُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِالْبَاطِلِ لِيُدْحِضُوا بِهِ الْحَقَّ ۖ وَاتَّخَذُوا آيَاتِي وَمَا أُنذِرُوا هُزُوًا
versets.tr: wa-mā nursilu l-mursalīna illā mubashshirīna wa-mundhirīna wa-yujādilu lladhīna kafarū bi-l-bāṭili li-yudḥiḍū bihi l-ḥaqqa wa-ttakhadhū āyātī wa-mā undhirū huzuwan
versets.trad: We send the messengers only as bearers of glad tidings and warners. Yet the disbelievers dispute with falsehood to refute the Truth thereby, and they take My signs and what they were warned of as mockery.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He sent the messengers in succession, supported them with proofs and arguments, commanded them to warn and to instill fear — and honor at the very heart of the trial of the charge. But some made their felicity by following them, others their wretchedness by opposing them. The mockery of the signs is the mark of the sealed heart.

versets.ar: وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّن ذُكِّرَ بِآيَاتِ رَبِّهِ فَأَعْرَضَ عَنْهَا وَنَسِيَ مَا قَدَّمَتْ يَدَاهُ ۚ إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ أَكِنَّةً أَن يَفْقَهُوهُ وَفِي آذَانِهِمْ وَقْرًا ۖ وَإِن تَدْعُهُمْ إِلَى الْهُدَىٰ فَلَن يَهْتَدُوا إِذًا أَبَدًا
versets.tr: wa-man aẓlamu mimman dhukkira bi-āyāti rabbihi fa-aʿraḍa ʿanhā wa-nasiya mā qaddamat yadāhu innā jaʿalnā ʿalā qulūbihim akinnatan an yafqahūhu wa-fī ādhānihim waqran wa-in tadʿuhum ilā l-hudā fa-lan yahtadū idhan abadan
versets.trad: Who is more wrongdoing than one who, reminded of the signs of his Lord, turns away from them and forgets what his own hands have sent forth? We have placed coverings over their hearts so that they do not understand it, and in their ears a heaviness. Though you call them to guidance, they will never be guided.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: none is more wrongdoing than one who, warned by the signs and by what he has seen and known — an affair set right, an affliction removed, a prayer answered, a fault corrected by a warning, an obedience rewarded by a sweetness in the heart — then finds himself before a new trial, and forgets what he was treated with, turns away from remembering it, and neglects what his hands have sent forth of good and evil; upon such a one is placed over the heart a veil, a heedlessness and a hardness, until the blessings of what was given him are cut off.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / أ ي ي

versets.ar: وَرَبُّكَ الْغَفُورُ ذُو الرَّحْمَةِ ۖ لَوْ يُؤَاخِذُهُم بِمَا كَسَبُوا لَعَجَّلَ لَهُمُ الْعَذَابَ ۚ بَل لَّهُم مَّوْعِدٌ لَّن يَجِدُوا مِن دُونِهِ مَوْئِلًا
versets.tr: wa-rabbuka l-ghafūru dhū r-raḥmati law yuʾākhidhuhum bi-mā kasabū la-ʿajjala lahumu l-ʿadhāba bal lahum mawʿidun lan yajidū min dūnihi mawʾilan
versets.trad: Your Lord is the Forgiving, the Possessor of mercy. Were He to take them to task for what they have earned, He would hasten the chastisement for them; but they have an appointed term from which they will find no escape.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He is “Forgiving” because He is “Possessor of mercy”; His preeternal mercy made forgiveness necessary for them. He is the Forgiver of the disobedient among His servants, and the Possessor of mercy toward all, setting right the state of each. Were He to chastise them according to their due, He would hasten the chastisement; but He defers it by the requirement of His wisdom.

versets.ar: وَتِلْكَ الْقُرَىٰ أَهْلَكْنَاهُمْ لَمَّا ظَلَمُوا وَجَعَلْنَا لِمَهْلِكِهِم مَّوْعِدًا
versets.tr: wa-tilka l-qurā ahlaknāhum lammā ẓalamū wa-jaʿalnā li-mahlikihim mawʿidan
versets.trad: And those towns — We destroyed them when they did wrong, and We set for their destruction an appointed time.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: because they did not give thanks for the bounties and did not endure patiently in the trials, We hastened their chastisement. And more subtly: because they were heedless of contemplating the decree and deprived of the breath of contentment (riḍā), We abandoned them to the darkness of their own contriving, and they sank into the valleys of their negligence.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِفَتَاهُ لَا أَبْرَحُ حَتَّىٰ أَبْلُغَ مَجْمَعَ الْبَحْرَيْنِ أَوْ أَمْضِيَ حُقُبًا
versets.tr: wa-idh qāla mūsā li-fatāhu lā abraḥu ḥattā ablugha majmaʿa l-baḥrayni aw amḍiya ḥuquban
versets.trad: And when Moses said to his young servant: “I will not cease until I reach the junction of the two seas, though I journey for ages.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: because Joshua's companionship with Moses was sincere, he merited the name of futuwwa — it is a name of honor, not of age. Moses' quest toward the junction of the two seas is the quest of the hidden knowledge: the seeker who sets out, even for ages, shows that the science of the Unseen is worth every effort.

mots.racine: ج م ع / ب ح ر
mots.sens: the junction of the two seas

mots.def: ḥuqub: a period of eighty years.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا بَلَغَا مَجْمَعَ بَيْنِهِمَا نَسِيَا حُوتَهُمَا فَاتَّخَذَ سَبِيلَهُ فِي الْبَحْرِ سَرَبًا
versets.tr: fa-lammā balaghā majmaʿa baynihimā nasiyā ḥūtahumā fa-ttakhadha sabīlahu fī l-baḥri saraban
versets.trad: But when they reached the junction between them, they forgot their fish, which made its way into the sea, slipping away.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He made the fish's entering the water the sign of al-Khiḍr's presence in that place, then cast forgetfulness upon them, so that it would be more perfect as a sign and farther from human choice. The forgetfulness itself is providential: it strips the meeting of all calculation and makes it pure gift.

mots.racine: ن س ي / ح و ت

mots.def: sarab: a path carved in the water.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا جَاوَزَا قَالَ لِفَتَاهُ آتِنَا غَدَاءَنَا لَقَدْ لَقِينَا مِن سَفَرِنَا هَٰذَا نَصَبًا
versets.tr: fa-lammā jāwazā qāla li-fatāhu ātinā ghadāʾanā laqad laqīnā min safarinā hādhā naṣaban
versets.trad: When they had passed beyond, he said to his servant: “Bring us our meal, for we have met with fatigue from this journey of ours.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives a fine comparison: Moses, in this journey, was the bearer of the burden — it was a journey of discipline and endured hardship, for he went seeking an increase of knowledge; and the state of seeking knowledge is a state of discipline and of borne hardship, which is why hunger reached him. But when he fasted thirty days awaiting to hear the Speech of God, neither hunger nor toil touched him — for that journey was toward God, and in it he was carried.

versets.ar: قَالَ أَرَأَيْتَ إِذْ أَوَيْنَا إِلَى الصَّخْرَةِ فَإِنِّي نَسِيتُ الْحُوتَ وَمَا أَنسَانِيهُ إِلَّا الشَّيْطَانُ أَنْ أَذْكُرَهُ ۚ وَاتَّخَذَ سَبِيلَهُ فِي الْبَحْرِ عَجَبًا
versets.tr: qāla a-raʾayta idh awaynā ilā ṣ-ṣakhrati fa-innī nasītu l-ḥūta wa-mā ansānīhu illā sh-shayṭānu an adhkurahu wa-ttakhadha sabīlahu fī l-baḥri ʿajaban
versets.trad: He said: “Did you see, when we took shelter at the rock, that I forgot the fish? None but Satan made me forget to mention it, and it took its way into the sea in a wondrous manner!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God cast forgetfulness upon Joshua so that the catching of the prey would come from his own effort; then “that is what we were seeking” designates the fish's entering the water — it was grilled and came back to life, which was a miracle. When they returned to the place where the fish had gained the sea, they found al-Khiḍr.

versets.ar: قَالَ ذَٰلِكَ مَا كُنَّا نَبْغِ ۚ فَارْتَدَّا عَلَىٰ آثَارِهِمَا قَصَصًا
versets.tr: qāla dhālika mā kunnā nabghi fa-rtaddā ʿalā āthārihimā qaṣaṣan
versets.trad: He said: “That is what we were seeking!” So they retraced their steps, following their tracks.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “that is what we were seeking” — it was the fish's entering the water that was the sign; so they retraced their steps, following their path exactly, until the place where the fish had entered the sea, and there they met al-Khiḍr. The meticulous following of the track (qaṣaṣ) figures the rigor of the seeker of the hidden knowledge.

versets.ar: فَوَجَدَا عَبْدًا مِّنْ عِبَادِنَا آتَيْنَاهُ رَحْمَةً مِّنْ عِندِنَا وَعَلَّمْنَاهُ مِن لَّدُنَّا عِلْمًا
versets.tr: fa-wajadā ʿabdan min ʿibādinā ātaynāhu raḥmatan min ʿindinā wa-ʿallamnāhu min ladunnā ʿilman
versets.trad: There they found a servant of Ours, to whom We had granted a mercy from Us and taught a knowledge from Our presence.
versets.commentaire: Here is a central pearl of Qushayrī: when God names a man “His servant,” He sets him among the elect; and when He says “My servant,” He sets him among the elite of the elite. On the knowledge from God: the knowledge “from Our presence” (min ladunnā) is what is attained by way of inspiration, without the toil of seeking; it is that by which the Real makes known the elite of His servants; it is a knowledge whose possessor finds no way to deny it, certain without holding proof — for the strongest of sciences is the farthest from demonstration.

versets.ar: قَالَ لَهُ مُوسَىٰ هَلْ أَتَّبِعُكَ عَلَىٰ أَن تُعَلِّمَنِ مِمَّا عُلِّمْتَ رُشْدًا
versets.tr: qāla lahu mūsā hal attabiʿuka ʿalā an tuʿallimani mimmā ʿullimta rushdan
versets.trad: Moses said to him: “May I follow you, that you teach me of the right guidance you have been taught?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Moses was delicate in his address, taking the path of asking leave, then expressing his aim: “that you teach me of what you have been taught”. And he notes: the knowledge with which al-Khiḍr was favored was not learned from any master nor any person — and what came from no one's teaching, how should another teach it? Therein lies the secret of the infused knowledge.

versets.ar: قَالَ إِنَّكَ لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِيَ صَبْرًا
versets.tr: qāla innaka lan tastaṭīʿa maʿiya ṣabran
versets.trad: He said: “You will surely not be able to bear with me patiently.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to the request full of gentleness comes a reply full of gentleness — but also a true warning. Al-Khiḍr knows that knowledge and decree are not contemplated by the same eye: Moses looks at things from the side of knowledge (ʿilm), while al-Khiḍr makes them pass from the side of the hidden judgment (ḥukm). Patience will fail where reason does not grasp the meaning.

versets.ar: وَكَيْفَ تَصْبِرُ عَلَىٰ مَا لَمْ تُحِطْ بِهِ خُبْرًا
versets.tr: wa-kayfa taṣbiru ʿalā mā lam tuḥiṭ bihi khubran
versets.trad: And how can you be patient over what you do not encompass in knowledge?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: al-Khiḍr touches the heart of the problem — how can one be patient over what one does not encompass in knowledge? Patience presupposes the sight of the meaning; where the meaning remains hidden, the appearance scandalizes. It is the trial of the disciple before the master whose acts confound outward knowledge.

versets.ar: قَالَ سَتَجِدُنِي إِن شَاءَ اللَّهُ صَابِرًا وَلَا أَعْصِي لَكَ أَمْرًا
versets.tr: qāla satajidunī in shāʾa llāhu ṣābiran wa-lā aʿṣī laka amran
versets.trad: Moses said: “You will find me patient, if God wills, and I will not disobey you in any command.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī observes a subtlety: Moses promised two things — patience and obedience; but patience he qualified with the exception by God's will: “you will find me patient if God wills” — and he was patient as far as he was found patient; whereas for obedience he said “I will not disobey” without exception, and there was the lapse. What he entrusted to God was kept; what he assured of himself was failed.

versets.ar: قَالَ فَإِنِ اتَّبَعْتَنِي فَلَا تَسْأَلْنِي عَن شَيْءٍ حَتَّىٰ أُحْدِثَ لَكَ مِنْهُ ذِكْرًا
versets.tr: qāla fa-ini ttabaʿtanī fa-lā tasʾalnī ʿan shayʾin ḥattā uḥditha laka minhu dhikran
versets.trad: He said: “If you follow me, do not ask me about anything until I myself mention it to you.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws here a rule of the Way: it does not belong to the aspirant to say “no” to his master, nor to the disciple to his teacher, nor to the layman to the learned who renders judgment in what he decides and rules. Trusting silence is the very condition of the transmission of the hidden knowledge.

mots.racine: ح د ث / ذ ك ر

versets.ar: فَانطَلَقَا حَتَّىٰ إِذَا رَكِبَا فِي السَّفِينَةِ خَرَقَهَا ۖ قَالَ أَخَرَقْتَهَا لِتُغْرِقَ أَهْلَهَا لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْئًا إِمْرًا
versets.tr: fa-nṭalaqā ḥattā idhā rakibā fī s-safīnati kharaqahā qāla a-kharaqtahā li-tughriqa ahlahā laqad jiʾta shayʾan imran
versets.trad: So they set off, until when they had boarded a ship, he made a breach in it. Moses said: “Have you breached it to drown its people? You have done a grievous thing!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when they boarded the boat, he holed it — and that was to preserve its owner, so that the king greedy for ships would not covet a holed boat. And “to drown its people”: al-Khiḍr did not aim at their drowning; Moses judged by the appearance of the damage, without seeing the hidden good it held.

mots.racine: ر ك ب / س ف ن

versets.ar: قَالَ أَلَمْ أَقُلْ إِنَّكَ لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِيَ صَبْرًا
versets.tr: qāla a-lam aqul innaka lan tastaṭīʿa maʿiya ṣabran
versets.trad: He said: “Did I not tell you that you would not be able to bear with me patiently?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sums up the root of the disagreement: you look at this from the side of knowledge, while We bring it about from the side of the hidden judgment. Moses' outward knowledge sees a crime; al-Khiḍr's inner ḥukm accomplishes a mercy. It is the gap between the apparent Law and the wisdom of the Unseen.

versets.ar: قَالَ لَا تُؤَاخِذْنِي بِمَا نَسِيتُ وَلَا تُرْهِقْنِي مِنْ أَمْرِي عُسْرًا
versets.tr: qāla lā tuʾākhidhnī bi-mā nasītu wa-lā turhiqnī min amrī ʿusran
versets.trad: Moses said: “Do not take me to task for what I forgot, and do not burden me with a hardship in my affair.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Moses claims what is the very condition of knowledge by saying “do not take me to task for my forgetfulness,” for the forgetful one does not come under the charge; and he supports it with “do not burden me with hardship” — for responsibility falls on one who has mastery of it, and the forgetful does not. Forgetfulness is the excuse of one who has not yet attained the station of perfect presence.

mots.sens: do not take me to task

versets.ar: فَانطَلَقَا حَتَّىٰ إِذَا لَقِيَا غُلَامًا فَقَتَلَهُ قَالَ أَقَتَلْتَ نَفْسًا زَكِيَّةً بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ لَّقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْئًا نُّكْرًا
versets.tr: fa-nṭalaqā ḥattā idhā laqiyā ghulāman fa-qatalahu qāla a-qatalta nafsan zakiyyatan bi-ghayri nafsin laqad jiʾta shayʾan nukran
versets.trad: So they set off, until when they met a boy, he killed him. Moses said: “Have you killed a pure soul, with no soul slain? You have done a reprehensible thing!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: by the law of knowledge, it was Moses' right to censure what outwardly appeared an injustice; but by what he knew of al-Khiḍr's state, his duty was to suspend his judgment until he knew whether the act fell under the forbidden or the permitted — and it was in that moment that the overturning of the habitual occurred. The appearance accuses; the hidden meaning justifies.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / غ ل م
ن ف س / ز ك و

versets.ar: قَالَ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكَ إِنَّكَ لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِيَ صَبْرًا
versets.tr: qāla a-lam aqul laka innaka lan tastaṭīʿa maʿiya ṣabran
versets.trad: He said: “Did I not tell you that you would not be able to bear with me patiently?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he repeated “you will not be able...” because he held to the condition of knowledge; but on the ground of unveiling, it was Moses who had set the condition. The repetition underscores that Moses, man of the Law and of prophecy, cannot help objecting where the meaning escapes him — and that patience before the incomprehensible is the highest of trials.

versets.ar: قَالَ إِن سَأَلْتُكَ عَن شَيْءٍ بَعْدَهَا فَلَا تُصَاحِبْنِي ۖ قَدْ بَلَغْتَ مِن لَّدُنِّي عُذْرًا
versets.tr: qāla in saʾaltuka ʿan shayʾin baʿdahā fa-lā tuṣāḥibnī qad balaghta min ladunnī ʿudhran
versets.trad: Moses said: “If I ask you about anything after this, then keep me company no more; you have received my excuse.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the bearing of the number: his transgression reached the number of three — and three is the last limit of the few and the first threshold of the many; after that, he found no further indulgence. This word reveals, he says, Qushayrī's conception of the utmost degree of failing still open to pardon.

versets.ar: فَانطَلَقَا حَتَّىٰ إِذَا أَتَيَا أَهْلَ قَرْيَةٍ اسْتَطْعَمَا أَهْلَهَا فَأَبَوْا أَن يُضَيِّفُوهُمَا فَوَجَدَا فِيهَا جِدَارًا يُرِيدُ أَن يَنقَضَّ فَأَقَامَهُ ۖ قَالَ لَوْ شِئْتَ لَاتَّخَذْتَ عَلَيْهِ أَجْرًا
versets.tr: fa-nṭalaqā ḥattā idhā atayā ahla qaryatin istaṭʿamā ahlahā fa-abaw an yuḍayyifūhumā fa-wajadā fīhā jidāran yurīdu an yanqaḍḍa fa-aqāmahu qāla law shiʾta la-ttakhadhta ʿalayhi ajran
versets.trad: So they set off, until when they came to the people of a town and asked them for food, they refused to host them. They found there a wall about to collapse, and he set it upright. Moses said: “If you wished, you could have taken a wage for it.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this journey was a journey of discipline, in which Moses was brought back to bearing hardship; otherwise, when he watered the daughters of Shuʿayb, the fatigue and hunger were greater — but then he was carried, and here he was the bearer. It is because, this time, his own portion intervened — to ask for a wage, to ask for food — that he was tried with separation. The master asked no wage for setting the wall upright.

mots.racine: ج د ر / ن ق ض

versets.ar: قَالَ هَٰذَا فِرَاقُ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنِكَ ۚ سَأُنَبِّئُكَ بِتَأْوِيلِ مَا لَمْ تَسْتَطِع عَّلَيْهِ صَبْرًا
versets.tr: qāla hādhā firāqu baynī wa-baynika sa-unabbiʾuka bi-taʾwīli mā lam tastaṭiʿ ʿalayhi ṣabran
versets.trad: He said: “This is the parting between you and me. I shall inform you of the interpretation of that over which you could not be patient.
themes: the explanation of the hidden meaning
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives several readings of the separation: so long as Moses questioned for the sake of others — for the poor men's boat, for the apparently unjust killing — al-Khiḍr did not leave him; but when, the third time, he came to speak of what his own portion was engaged in (the asking for food), he was struck with separation. And again: just as Moses loved al-Khiḍr's company out of desire for an increase of knowledge, al-Khiḍr loved to leave Moses' company, preferring solitude with God over creatures.

versets.ar: أَمَّا السَّفِينَةُ فَكَانَتْ لِمَسَاكِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ فِي الْبَحْرِ فَأَرَدتُّ أَنْ أَعِيبَهَا وَكَانَ وَرَاءَهُم مَّلِكٌ يَأْخُذُ كُلَّ سَفِينَةٍ غَصْبًا
versets.tr: ammā s-safīnatu fa-kānat li-masākīna yaʿmalūna fī l-baḥri fa-aradtu an aʿībahā wa-kāna warāʾahum malikun yaʾkhudhu kulla safīnatin ghaṣban
versets.trad: As for the ship, it belonged to poor men working on the sea. I wished to damage it, for behind them was a king who seized every ship by force.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when al-Khiḍr parted from Moses, he did not wish to leave in Moses' heart the shadow of an objection; he dispelled it by disclosing to him the hidden state: the secret in his holing the boat was its preservation and its remaining with its people, the despoiling king not coveting a damaged boat. Its survival damaged was better for them than its perfection confiscated.

versets.ar: وَأَمَّا الْغُلَامُ فَكَانَ أَبَوَاهُ مُؤْمِنَيْنِ فَخَشِينَا أَن يُرْهِقَهُمَا طُغْيَانًا وَكُفْرًا
versets.tr: wa-ammā l-ghulāmu fa-kāna abawāhu muʾminayni fa-khashīnā an yurhiqahumā ṭughyānan wa-kufran
versets.trad: As for the boy, his parents were believers, and we feared he would burden them with rebellion and unbelief.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He showed him that the killing of the boy was decreed beforehand by God's prior knowledge, for in his survival was a trial for his parents, and in the gift of a replacement a felicity for them. What seems cruelty is in truth mercy: to preserve the parents' faith by removing the child who would have dragged them into evil.

mots.racine: أ ب و / أ م ن
ط غ ي / ك ف ر

versets.ar: فَأَرَدْنَا أَن يُبْدِلَهُمَا رَبُّهُمَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهُ زَكَاةً وَأَقْرَبَ رُحْمًا
versets.tr: fa-aradnā an yubdilahumā rabbuhumā khayran minhu zakātan wa-aqraba ruḥman
versets.trad: So we wished that their Lord would give them in exchange one better than him in purity and nearer in affection.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God willed to substitute for the child a better one, purer and nearer in tenderness. The hidden meaning of the act is pure mercy: what Moses judged a crime was the opening of a higher good. The wisdom of the Unseen destroys only to give better; what it removes with one hand, it restores purer with the other.

mots.racine: خ ي ر / ز ك و
ق ر ب / ر ح م

versets.ar: وَأَمَّا الْجِدَارُ فَكَانَ لِغُلَامَيْنِ يَتِيمَيْنِ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ وَكَانَ تَحْتَهُ كَنزٌ لَّهُمَا وَكَانَ أَبُوهُمَا صَالِحًا فَأَرَادَ رَبُّكَ أَن يَبْلُغَا أَشُدَّهُمَا وَيَسْتَخْرِجَا كَنزَهُمَا رَحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ ۚ وَمَا فَعَلْتُهُ عَنْ أَمْرِي ۚ ذَٰلِكَ تَأْوِيلُ مَا لَمْ تَسْطِع عَّلَيْهِ صَبْرًا
versets.tr: wa-ammā l-jidāru fa-kāna li-ghulāmayni yatīmayni fī l-madīnati wa-kāna taḥtahu kanzun lahumā wa-kāna abūhumā ṣāliḥan fa-arāda rabbuka an yablughā ashuddahumā wa-yastakhrijā kanzahumā raḥmatan min rabbika wa-mā faʿaltuhu ʿan amrī dhālika taʾwīlu mā lam tasṭiʿ ʿalayhi ṣabran
versets.trad: As for the wall, it belonged to two orphan boys in the city, and beneath it was a treasure for them; their father had been a righteous man, and your Lord willed that they reach maturity and bring out their treasure as a mercy from your Lord. I did not do it of my own accord. That is the interpretation of what you could not bear with patience.”
themes: “I did it not of my own accord”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: as for the setting upright of the wall, it was to preserve the treasure of the two orphans and to forgo seeking a benefit from creatures. And the verse culminates on the decisive word: “I did not do it of my own accord” — the true agent acts only by God; the saint is but the passage-place of the decree, never its source. The father's righteousness saves the sons: a man's uprightness protects his offspring beyond his death.

mots.racine: ف ع ل / أ م ر
mots.sens: I did it not of my own accord

versets.ar: وَيَسْأَلُونَكَ عَن ذِي الْقَرْنَيْنِ ۖ قُلْ سَأَتْلُو عَلَيْكُم مِّنْهُ ذِكْرًا
versets.tr: wa-yasʾalūnaka ʿan dhī l-qarnayni qul sa-atlū ʿalaykum minhu dhikran
versets.trad: They ask you about Dhū-l-Qarnayn. Say: “I shall recite to you a remembrance of him.”
themes: the account of the just king
versets.commentaire: The account of Dhū-l-Qarnayn opens as an answer to the questioners. For Qushayrī, the sovereign who traverses the earth from west to east figures the guided soul crossing the states: he receives power not for himself but to render justice, and his journey is that of the knowledge which embraces the horizons without clinging to any.

mots.def: dhū l-qarnayn: of the two horns.

versets.ar: إِنَّا مَكَّنَّا لَهُ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَآتَيْنَاهُ مِن كُلِّ شَيْءٍ سَبَبًا
versets.tr: innā makkannā lahu fī l-arḍi wa-ātaynāhu min kulli shayʾin sababan
versets.trad: We established him firmly on the earth and gave him a means to all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees in the establishment of Dhū-l-Qarnayn and the “means” to all things the gift of causes set in the service of a right end: power is grace only when it is employed according to the will of the One who gives it. He who receives the means and refers them to their Source sanctifies them; he who appropriates them corrupts them.

versets.ar: فَأَتْبَعَ سَبَبًا
versets.tr: fa-atbaʿa sababan
versets.trad: So he followed a way,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Dhū-l-Qarnayn “followed a means” — he took the causes God had opened to him. To follow the sabab is to journey by the ways offered without halting at them, crossing through them toward their goal. The traveler of the Way uses the means as mounts, not as dwellings.

versets.ar: حَتَّىٰ إِذَا بَلَغَ مَغْرِبَ الشَّمْسِ وَجَدَهَا تَغْرُبُ فِي عَيْنٍ حَمِئَةٍ وَوَجَدَ عِندَهَا قَوْمًا ۗ قُلْنَا يَا ذَا الْقَرْنَيْنِ إِمَّا أَن تُعَذِّبَ وَإِمَّا أَن تَتَّخِذَ فِيهِمْ حُسْنًا
versets.tr: ḥattā idhā balagha maghriba sh-shamsi wajadahā taghrubu fī ʿaynin ḥamiʾatin wa-wajada ʿindahā qawman qulnā yā dhā l-qarnayni immā an tuʿadhdhiba wa-immā an tattakhidha fīhim ḥusnan
