versets.trad: until when he reached the setting of the sun, he found it setting in a murky spring, and found near it a people. We said: “O Dhū-l-Qarnayn, either you punish them or you treat them with kindness.”
versets.commentaire: The choice offered — to punish or to do good — is, for the Sufi reading, the choice left to the just ruler between rigor and grace. Qushayrī, in his treatment of the horizons, sees in the setting the place of occultation and in the rising that of unveiling: the sage embraces both poles without losing himself, judging each by its due.

mots.racine: غ ر ب / ش م س
ع ي ن / ح م أ

versets.ar: قَالَ أَمَّا مَن ظَلَمَ فَسَوْفَ نُعَذِّبُهُ ثُمَّ يُرَدُّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِ فَيُعَذِّبُهُ عَذَابًا نُّكْرًا
versets.tr: qāla ammā man ẓalama fa-sawfa nuʿadhdhibuhu thumma yuraddu ilā rabbihi fa-yuʿadhdhibuhu ʿadhāban nukran
versets.trad: He said: “As for him who does wrong, we shall punish him, then he will be returned to his Lord, who will chastise him with a terrible chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Dhū-l-Qarnayn distinguishes the lot of the wrongdoer: punishment here below, then chastisement with God. His justice articulates the apparent order and the hidden order — he punishes by the Law while referring the ultimate verdict to God. The just ruler, in the Sufi reading, does not usurp the final judgment: he anticipates it without confiscating it.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ن ك ر

versets.ar: وَأَمَّا مَنْ آمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا فَلَهُ جَزَاءً الْحُسْنَىٰ ۖ وَسَنَقُولُ لَهُ مِنْ أَمْرِنَا يُسْرًا
versets.tr: wa-ammā man āmana wa-ʿamila ṣāliḥan fa-lahu jazāʾan il-ḥusnā wa-sa-naqūlu lahu min amrinā yusran
versets.trad: But as for him who believes and does righteousness, he shall have the fairest reward, and we shall speak to him an easy command.”
versets.commentaire: To the believer, Dhū-l-Qarnayn promises the fairest reward and an easy command. The sage's justice is a gentle justice toward whoever does good: it does not weigh down the burden but lightens it. For Qushayrī, true power is recognized by its making the way easy for the people of faith.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / ح س ن

versets.ar: ثُمَّ أَتْبَعَ سَبَبًا
versets.tr: thumma atbaʿa sababan
versets.trad: Then he followed a way,
versets.commentaire: Again, Dhū-l-Qarnayn “followed a means”: the journeying resumes, from horizon to horizon. The traveler of God settles nowhere; each stage crossed calls forth another. The power received unfolds in continuous service, never in satisfied repose.

mots.def: atbaʿa: to take up a way.

versets.ar: حَتَّىٰ إِذَا بَلَغَ مَطْلِعَ الشَّمْسِ وَجَدَهَا تَطْلُعُ عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ لَّمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُم مِّن دُونِهَا سِتْرًا
versets.tr: ḥattā idhā balagha maṭliʿa sh-shamsi wajadahā taṭluʿu ʿalā qawmin lam najʿal lahum min dūnihā sitran
versets.trad: until when he reached the rising of the sun, he found it rising upon a people for whom We had made no shelter against it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives here a beautiful allegory: some are the people of the rising of the sun, over whom the length of their day prevails; others, the people of the setting, over whom the hiding of their sun prevails. So it is with people at the rising of the sun of Oneness: over some the rising of their suns prevails, and presence is their attribute, contemplation their state, oneness their due; the others have of the suns of Oneness only the slenderest portion and the meanest share.

mots.racine: ط ل ع / ش م س

versets.ar: كَذَٰلِكَ وَقَدْ أَحَطْنَا بِمَا لَدَيْهِ خُبْرًا
versets.tr: kadhālika wa-qad aḥaṭnā bi-mā ladayhi khubran
versets.trad: Thus it was, and We encompassed in knowledge all that was with him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God encompasses in His knowledge all that concerns Dhū-l-Qarnayn — his states, his means, his journeying. The divine knowledge encompasses (aḥāṭa) what human knowledge cannot reach. The sage acts under the gaze of the One who knows all: his power is never outside the encompassing knowledge of God.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ أَتْبَعَ سَبَبًا
versets.tr: thumma atbaʿa sababan
versets.trad: Then he followed a way,
versets.commentaire: A third time, Dhū-l-Qarnayn resumes the road. The triple repetition of “he followed a means” scans a journeying without end: from west to east, then toward the barrier. The servant established by God ceases not to serve; his power is spent in beneficence, not in enjoyment.

versets.ar: حَتَّىٰ إِذَا بَلَغَ بَيْنَ السَّدَّيْنِ وَجَدَ مِن دُونِهِمَا قَوْمًا لَّا يَكَادُونَ يَفْقَهُونَ قَوْلًا
versets.tr: ḥattā idhā balagha bayna s-saddayni wajada min dūnihimā qawman lā yakādūna yafqahūna qawlan
versets.trad: until when he reached a place between the two barriers, he found beyond them a people who could scarcely understand a word.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this people oriented themselves only to their own tongue and did not understand the speech of others; they resorted to their own expressions to set forth their affair, and laid before him their grievance concerning Gog and Magog. The people who “scarcely understand a word” figure those enclosed within their sole horizon, unable to hear the language of the Spirit.

mots.def: kāda: to be on the point of.

mots.racine: ف ق ه / ق و ل

versets.ar: قَالُوا يَا ذَا الْقَرْنَيْنِ إِنَّ يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ مُفْسِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَهَلْ نَجْعَلُ لَكَ خَرْجًا عَلَىٰ أَن تَجْعَلَ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَهُمْ سَدًّا
versets.tr: qālū yā dhā l-qarnayni inna yaʾjūja wa-maʾjūja mufsidūna fī l-arḍi fa-hal najʿalu laka kharjan ʿalā an tajʿala baynanā wa-baynahum saddan
versets.trad: They said: “O Dhū-l-Qarnayn, Gog and Magog spread corruption on the earth. May we pay you a tribute so that you set a barrier between us and them?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they raised to him their complaint concerning Gog and Magog and guaranteed him a tribute to pay; he granted their request and fulfilled their wish, but took not from them what they had promised of revenue — seeing that it was incumbent upon him to protect them according to his capacity. The help of the just one is not sold: it is owed by duty, not by contract.

versets.ar: قَالَ مَا مَكَّنِّي فِيهِ رَبِّي خَيْرٌ فَأَعِينُونِي بِقُوَّةٍ أَجْعَلْ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ رَدْمًا
versets.tr: qāla mā makkannī fīhi rabbī khayrun fa-aʿīnūnī bi-quwwatin ajʿal baynakum wa-baynahum radman
versets.trad: He said: “What my Lord has established me in is better. So help me with strength, and I shall set a rampart between you and them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Dhū-l-Qarnayn refuses the tribute — “what my Lord has empowered me with is better” — and asks only their effort. The saint established by God seeks no gain from creatures; he sets his received power in the service of the common good and asks of men only their cooperation. Whoever refers his power to God does not make trade of it with men.

mots.racine: ع و ن / ق و ي

versets.ar: آتُونِي زُبَرَ الْحَدِيدِ ۖ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا سَاوَىٰ بَيْنَ الصَّدَفَيْنِ قَالَ انفُخُوا ۖ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا جَعَلَهُ نَارًا قَالَ آتُونِي أُفْرِغْ عَلَيْهِ قِطْرًا
versets.tr: ātūnī zubara l-ḥadīdi ḥattā idhā sāwā bayna ṣ-ṣadafayni qāla nfukhū ḥattā idhā jaʿalahu nāran qāla ātūnī ufrigh ʿalayhi qiṭran
versets.trad: Bring me blocks of iron!” When he had leveled the space between the two cliffs, he said: “Blow!” And when he had made it fire, he said: “Bring me molten brass to pour over it.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he resorted to their help for what he needed — “bring me blocks of iron”; and when they had done what he commanded and blown the fire, he built the barrier between the two flanks of the mountain. The saint's work is accomplished through the ordained means, each cause in its place: the iron, the blowing, the brass — an image of the mastery that orders the causes toward their end.

mots.racine: ز ب ر / ح د د

versets.ar: فَمَا اسْطَاعُوا أَن يَظْهَرُوهُ وَمَا اسْتَطَاعُوا لَهُ نَقْبًا
versets.tr: fa-mā sṭāʿū an yaẓharūhu wa-mā staṭāʿū lahu naqban
versets.trad: So they could neither scale it nor pierce through it.
versets.commentaire: The rampart holds: neither scaled nor pierced. For Qushayrī, the barrier that the just one raises by the strength received from God is firm so long as the decree lasts. The work done for God and by the means He ordains resists, where the work of corruptors fails. The protection accomplished is mercy, but it remains suspended on the divine will.

versets.ar: قَالَ هَٰذَا رَحْمَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّي ۖ فَإِذَا جَاءَ وَعْدُ رَبِّي جَعَلَهُ دَكَّاءَ ۖ وَكَانَ وَعْدُ رَبِّي حَقًّا
versets.tr: qāla hādhā raḥmatun min rabbī fa-idhā jāʾa waʿdu rabbī jaʿalahu dakkāʾa wa-kāna waʿdu rabbī ḥaqqan
versets.trad: He said: “This is a mercy from my Lord! But when the promise of my Lord comes, He will level it to the ground. The promise of my Lord is true.”
themes: the work, a mercy of God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he announced that this work would last only until God permits it to come forth, warding off harm from people until the term fixed in the decree; after which will come what God wills. And the Real has made clear that the coming forth of Gog and Magog from behind their barrier is one of the signs of the Hour. Dhū-l-Qarnayn refers his work not to himself but to the mercy of his Lord: such is the mark of the true servant.

mots.racine: ر ح م / ر ب ب
و ع د / ح ق ق

versets.ar: وَتَرَكْنَا بَعْضَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَمُوجُ فِي بَعْضٍ ۖ وَنُفِخَ فِي الصُّورِ فَجَمَعْنَاهُمْ جَمْعًا
versets.tr: wa-taraknā baʿḍahum yawmaʾidhin yamūju fī baʿḍin wa-nufikha fī ṣ-ṣūri fa-jamaʿnāhum jamʿan
versets.trad: On that Day We shall leave them surging against one another; and the trumpet will be blown, and We shall gather them all together.
versets.commentaire: The surging of people against one another and the blast of the trumpet announce the great gathering. For Qushayrī, this final upheaval is the term at which every barrier gives way and each is brought back before God. The order of the world, for a moment held by the ramparts and the pillars, dissolves for the Day of the universal summons. The world held a moment by ramparts and pillars yields wholly to God.

mots.racine: ن ف خ / ص و ر

versets.ar: وَعَرَضْنَا جَهَنَّمَ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْكَافِرِينَ عَرْضًا
versets.tr: wa-ʿaraḍnā jahannama yawmaʾidhin li-l-kāfirīna ʿarḍan
versets.trad: On that Day We shall present Hell, fully displayed, to the disbelievers,
versets.commentaire: Hell “displayed” without veil to the disbelievers: it is the direct confrontation with what they denied. For Qushayrī, the harshest chastisement is not so much the Fire as the unveiling — to see face to face the reality refused. Those who veiled their eyes from the Remembrance now see what they could not bear to hear. Those who veiled their eyes from the Remembrance now see what they could not bear to hear.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ كَانَتْ أَعْيُنُهُمْ فِي غِطَاءٍ عَن ذِكْرِي وَكَانُوا لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ سَمْعًا
versets.tr: alladhīna kānat aʿyunuhum fī ghiṭāʾin ʿan dhikrī wa-kānū lā yastaṭīʿūna samʿan
versets.trad: those whose eyes were under a covering from My remembrance and who could not bear to hear.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they looked with the eyes of their heads because they had lost the sight of the heart, that of consideration and inference; they had not the hearing of response because they were deprived of divine assistance — the charge fell upon them but the unveiling did not second them. “They could not hear”: because they were deprived, from Him, of the making-to-hear, and so could not accept.

mots.racine: ع ي ن / غ ط و

versets.ar: أَفَحَسِبَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَن يَتَّخِذُوا عِبَادِي مِن دُونِي أَوْلِيَاءَ ۚ إِنَّا أَعْتَدْنَا جَهَنَّمَ لِلْكَافِرِينَ نُزُلًا
versets.tr: a-fa-ḥasiba lladhīna kafarū an yattakhidhū ʿibādī min dūnī awliyāʾa innā aʿtadnā jahannama li-l-kāfirīna nuzulan
versets.trad: Do the disbelievers think they can take My servants as protectors besides Me? Indeed We have prepared Hell as a dwelling for the disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they imagined that their conduct would profit them according to their belief, and held their idols worthy of veneration, saying “we worship them only that they may bring us nearer to God,” “thinking they did well” — then there appeared to them from God what they did not reckon. To take as allies other than God, even His own servants turned away from Him, is to consign oneself to the dwelling of the Fire.

versets.ar: قُلْ هَلْ نُنَبِّئُكُم بِالْأَخْسَرِينَ أَعْمَالًا
versets.tr: qul hal nunabbiʾukum bi-l-akhsarīna aʿmālan
versets.trad: Say: “Shall We tell you of the greatest losers in deeds,
versets.commentaire: The announcement of the “greatest losers in deeds” introduces the supreme warning: there are men whose very effort ruins them. For Qushayrī, the worst of strayings is the one that takes itself for rectitude. The rhetorical question prepares the unveiling of this tragedy: to think one does well while perishing.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُمْ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَهُمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ يُحْسِنُونَ صُنْعًا
versets.tr: alladhīna ḍalla saʿyuhum fī l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-hum yaḥsabūna annahum yuḥsinūna ṣunʿan
versets.trad: those whose effort goes astray in the life of this world while they think they are doing good?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their effort went astray because they worked for other than God — and what is for other than God does not profit. And he specifies: these are they who joined ostentation to their deeds, tinged their states with self-complacency, and annulled their beneficence by the gaze upon self or by the reproach of the gift; these are they who look upon their deeds with the eye of overestimation. The illusion of doing well is the soul's subtlest trap.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / س ع ي
mots.def: ḍalla: to go astray; saʿy: striving.

mots.racine: ح س ن / ص ن ع

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآيَاتِ رَبِّهِمْ وَلِقَائِهِ فَحَبِطَتْ أَعْمَالُهُمْ فَلَا نُقِيمُ لَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَزْنًا
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna kafarū bi-āyāti rabbihim wa-liqāʾihi fa-ḥabiṭat aʿmāluhum fa-lā nuqīmu lahum yawma l-qiyāmati waznan
versets.trad: Those are they who disbelieve in the signs of their Lord and in meeting Him, so their deeds are in vain, and We shall assign them no weight on the Day of Resurrection.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they were blind to the contemplation of the Reality and remained in the darkness of denial; conjectures and surmises scattered them, they had no clear sight, their hearts settled on no certain creed — and so they have in the hereafter neither weight nor worth; today they are like cattle, and tomorrow they fall, cast down. The deed without faith or vision weighs nothing in the balance.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / أ ي ي
ح ب ط / ع م ل
mots.def: ḥabiṭa: to be reduced to nought.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ جَزَاؤُهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ بِمَا كَفَرُوا وَاتَّخَذُوا آيَاتِي وَرُسُلِي هُزُوًا
versets.tr: dhālika jazāʾuhum jahannamu bi-mā kafarū wa-ttakhadhū āyātī wa-rusulī huzuwan
versets.trad: That is their recompense — Hell — because they disbelieved and took My signs and My messengers in mockery.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses their lot in an antithesis: today they are in the chastisement of denial, tomorrow in the chastisement of rejection; today in the humiliation of separation, tomorrow in the pain of burning. The mockery of the signs and the messengers, sin of pride, calls forth Hell as recompense: contempt of the sacred turns to fire.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ كَانَتْ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتُ الْفِرْدَوْسِ نُزُلًا
versets.tr: inna lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti kānat lahum jannātu l-firdawsi nuzulan
versets.trad: Indeed those who believe and do righteous deeds shall have the Gardens of Paradise as their dwelling,
versets.commentaire: Here is a pearl of Qushayrī: they have gardens anticipated in secret, and gardens deferred in the open; today the gardens of union, tomorrow the gardens of favor; today the gardens of knowledge, tomorrow the gardens of good-pleasure. The Firdaws of the believers is not only future promise: it begins here below in the secret of the united heart.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ع م ل
ج ن ن / ف ر د س

versets.ar: خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا لَا يَبْغُونَ عَنْهَا حِوَلًا
versets.tr: khālidīna fīhā lā yabghūna ʿanhā ḥiwalan
versets.trad: abiding therein forever, desiring no change from it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He has taught us that what He will grant them tomorrow will be permanent; they will not be deprived of their graces, nor depart from their states — forever in the Garden, with no expulsion; forever the Vision, with no veil. The height of felicity is to desire no change: the heart at last established in unbroken contemplation.

versets.ar: قُل لَّوْ كَانَ الْبَحْرُ مِدَادًا لِّكَلِمَاتِ رَبِّي لَنَفِدَ الْبَحْرُ قَبْلَ أَن تَنفَدَ كَلِمَاتُ رَبِّي وَلَوْ جِئْنَا بِمِثْلِهِ مَدَدًا
versets.tr: qul law kāna l-baḥru midādan li-kalimāti rabbī la-nafida l-baḥru qabla an tanfada kalimātu rabbī wa-law jiʾnā bi-mithlihi madadan
versets.trad: Say: “If the sea were ink for the words of my Lord, the sea would run dry before the words of my Lord were spent, even if We brought the like of it as replenishment.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the meanings of God's words cannot be numbered, for they have no end: the objects of the eternal attribute are without term, like the known things of the Real, His powers and all the objects of His attributes. Now that which is created — the sea become ink — cannot exhaust what is infinite, however vast. The sea, being created, has a term; the words, being of the eternal attribute, have none.

mots.racine: ك ل م / ر ب ب

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنَّمَا أَنَا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ يُوحَىٰ إِلَيَّ أَنَّمَا إِلَٰهُكُمْ إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ ۖ فَمَن كَانَ يَرْجُو لِقَاءَ رَبِّهِ فَلْيَعْمَلْ عَمَلًا صَالِحًا وَلَا يُشْرِكْ بِعِبَادَةِ رَبِّهِ أَحَدًا
versets.tr: qul innamā anā basharun mithlukum yūḥā ilayya annamā ilāhukum ilāhun wāḥidun fa-man kāna yarjū liqāʾa rabbihi fa-l-yaʿmal ʿamalan ṣāliḥan wa-lā yushrik bi-ʿibādati rabbihi aḥadan
versets.trad: Say: “I am only a mortal like you; it is revealed to me that your God is one God. So whoever hopes for the meeting with his Lord, let him do righteous deeds and associate none in the worship of his Lord.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī seals the sūra on a pearl of hope: “whoever hopes for the meeting with his Lord” — to take this word as fear of chastisement and hope of reward is sound, but to leave it on its literal sense is better: all believers hope for the meeting with God, and the knower hopes for His meeting and His vision. The righteous deed by which he reaches it is his patience in the burnings of his longing, and the purity of his act; and “let him associate none” means: let him not contemplate his own deed, nor overestimate it, and let him disown his strength and power. The difference between the Prophet and men is not in form but in election (iṣṭifāʾ) by Revelation.

mots.racine: ب ش ر / م ث ل
ر ج و / ل ق ي
mots.sens: hopes for the meeting with his Lord
